audit.area: Phase 0 — verification freeze (content-hash drift gate) + per-guard regression-snapshot suite
audit.currentState:
The verifier surface is the app's deterministic liar-catcher and it ALREADY EXISTS, is wired, green, and well-documented. Inventory of the two directories:

(A) src/lib/lab-engine/agents/verification/ — 12 source files (.ts, ~9,300 LOC), each carrying a file-level comment naming the EXACT production run that triggered its guard:
  - claim-extractor.ts (556 LOC) — claim harvesting; self-authored-label exclusions (Anura run: 3/7 paid-media unsupported claims were its own funnel names), display-URL exemption (run harness-ramp-2e3adf77: 8 bare homepages hard-failed Competitor). Imported only by other verifiers (transitively wired).
  - claim-source-verifier.ts (1183 LOC) — paid-media plan FABRICATION/MIS_ATTRIBUTION/FABRICATED_QUOTE judge (run 73dfbc0d). Imported by run-section.ts.
  - creative-truth-gate.ts (355 LOC) — dropConfessedExemplarQuotes + stripUngroundedNamedEntityMetrics (W6 Manus program; legal exposure of unsupported metric on a named person in deployable ad copy).
  - downgrade-coverage.ts (453 LOC) — 7 per-section coverage reconcilers (§4.6/Gap B).
  - evidence-support.ts (1653 LOC, hottest file: 16 commits/14d) — redactUnsupportedNumericClaims, stripMisattributedQuoteAttributions, stripModelAuthoredVerifiedMarkers, inline-marker budget, getMaxUnsupportedAllowed (LAB_VERIFIER_MAX_UNSUPPORTED). Imported by 5 prod modules.
  - grounded-buyer-unit.ts (115 LOC) — BuyerICP grounding (run b0d12b45: found 3 real champions, shipped 0 personas).
  - numeric-coherence.ts (963 LOC) — enforceNumericCoherence + scrubInternalJargon (run 8081e646 shipped 'CHANNEL POLICY'/'verifiedCount=0' in hero copy; '~5,900 searches/month' contradicting a table summing 7,300; run 314d5f02, d838ed4e).
  - provenance-gate.ts (1121 LOC) — stripExemplarEchoes (8081e646 shipped Ramp OCR value-prop in Airtable hooks), downgradeUnpermalinkedVerbatimQuotes, scrubQuoteEmails (shipped employee email in a VoC quote), stripPlaceholderSourceUrls.
  - quote-admission.ts (447 LOC) — chrome-strip, truncation, permalink admission, cross-block dedup (run c9bc2056).
  - source-liveness.ts (1475 LOC) — http containment/liveness + downgrade-not-delete (runs c9bc2056, d838ed4e).
  - structural-verifier.ts (904 LOC) — the entailment/claim verifier; the ONLY function the deterministic eval gate exercises.
  - types.ts (56 LOC) — Claim/ClaimVerdict/VerificationReport shapes (shared contract).

(B) src/lib/lab-engine/verification/ — deck-ledger-gate.ts (381 LOC). NOTE: zero production importers — only scripts/zz-prove-p0-spine.ts, scripts/zz-full-run-harness.ts, scripts/lib/persona-ledger-check.ts and its 2 test files reference it. Not on the live commit path.

Production choke point: src/lib/lab-engine/agents/run-section.ts chains ~20 strip/gate functions in a FIXED order around lines 5330-5510 (the 'annotateEvidenceSupportReview' choke point) — strip-misattributed → dropConfessedExemplar → stripExemplarEcho → downgradeUnpermalinked → scrubQuoteEmails → stripPlaceholderUrls → sourceLiveness → stripUnverified/Uncontained → dedupeQuotes → scrubBodyJargon → enforceNumericCoherence → stripUngroundedNamedEntityMetrics/redactUnsupportedNumeric → stripModelAuthoredVerifiedMarkers → scrubInternalJargon.

EXISTING test infra (all green): 
  - Unit tests: src/lib/lab-engine/agents/verification/__tests__/*.test.ts — one file per verifier, 231 cases total (evidence-support 43, provenance-gate 32, source-liveness 32, numeric-coherence 24, quote-admission 24, structural-verifier 16, claim-extractor 13, claim-source-verifier 13, downgrade-coverage 8, creative-truth-gate 8, grounded-buyer-unit 8). MANY already cite the triggering run id inline (8081e646, c9bc2056, b0d12b45, d838ed4e, 314d5f02, 73dfbc0d) — the regression spec partly exists already, just not systematized.
  - Eval gate: src/lib/lab-engine/agents/verification/__evals__/verifier.eval.test.ts — JSON-fixture-driven (import.meta.glob over ./fixtures/*.json), asserts EXACTLY 15 fixtures, runs structuralVerifier + checkRequiredEvidenceClasses, checks verifiedCount/unsupportedCount/requiredClassesSatisfied. Fixtures include 4 synthetic-fabricated (synthetic-fabricated-price.json, synthetic-fabricated-quote.json, synthetic-buyer-fabricated-persona.json, synthetic-missing-competitor.json) + 11 real-subject (ramp/vanta/webflow/notion).
  - deck-ledger-gate has its own deck-ledger-gate.test.ts (8) + deck-ledger-gate.fixture.test.ts (2).
  - Full baseline: 247 tests pass across 14 files in 1.7s.

Test commands: package.json has test:run ('NODE_ENV=test vitest run') and test:gate (node --test on 5 scripts/*.gate.test.mjs — does NOT run vitest verifier tests). No .github/workflows/ exists (no CI; gates run locally/pre-commit). node:crypto/createHash already used in 6 repo files (run-section.ts, lab-section-job.ts, dispatch-research.ts, etc.) — hash manifest needs no new dependency. No barrel index.ts in either dir (manifest can glob *.ts directly). No content-hash/freeze/drift gate and no toMatchSnapshot anywhere in the verification tree today.

tasks.title: Build the SHA-256 content-hash drift gate as an ACKNOWLEDGEMENT gate (not an immutability lock)
tasks.what: Add scripts/lib/verifier-freeze-manifest.ts that lists the 13 frozen source files (the 12 in agents/verification/ + verification/deck-ledger-gate.ts) and computes a SHA-256 per file via node:crypto createHash (already used in 6 repo files — no new dep; glob the dirs since there is no barrel index.ts). Generate a committed manifest src/lib/lab-engine/agents/verification/__freeze__/freeze-manifest.json mapping relativePath->sha256. Add a vitest drift test src/lib/lab-engine/agents/verification/__freeze__/freeze-drift.test.ts that recomputes hashes and asserts they equal the manifest. CRITICAL DESIGN (grounded in churn data: 37 commits in 14d, evidence-support.ts touched 16x): the gate must NOT forbid edits — it fails ONLY when a hash diverges, with an error message instructing the editor to run `npm run freeze:verifiers -- --regenerate` and commit the new manifest IN THE SAME COMMIT. This makes every verifier edit a conscious, reviewed act ('you touched a liar-catcher, prove you meant to') rather than blocking the active surface. Add npm scripts freeze:verifiers (check) and the --regenerate flag.
tasks.acceptance: `npm run freeze:verifiers` exits 0 on a clean tree; mutating any one byte of any of the 13 files makes the drift test FAIL with a message naming the file and the regenerate command; running --regenerate then re-running exits 0. Manifest covers exactly the 13 non-test .ts files (verified by a test asserting manifest key-count === globbed file count, so a NEW verifier file added without a manifest entry also fails).

tasks.title: Decide + lock the exact frozen-surface file set (resolve 3 borderline members)
tasks.what: Before writing the manifest, make 3 inclusion calls explicit and record them in AGENTS.md: (1) types.ts — INCLUDE (shared Claim/ClaimVerdict/VerificationReport contract; a silent shape change breaks every guard). (2) verification/deck-ledger-gate.ts — INCLUDE in the manifest but FLAG it has zero production importers (scripts/tests only); the freeze documents that it is currently off the live commit path so Phase 2 does not assume it runs. (3) src/lib/lab-engine/sections/required-evidence.ts — this is the SECOND function the eval gate exercises (checkRequiredEvidenceClasses) yet lives outside both verification dirs; decide whether the freeze surface is 'the two verification dirs' (exclude required-evidence) or 'the deterministic-gate surface' (include it). Recommend INCLUDE required-evidence to avoid a freeze that is blind to half the eval gate. Document the chosen boundary as the binding definition the manifest globs against.
tasks.acceptance: AGENTS.md gains a 'Frozen surface (Phase 0)' section listing every file in/out of the manifest with a one-line rationale each; deck-ledger-gate's zero-prod-importer status is stated; the required-evidence include/exclude decision is written down and the manifest matches it exactly.

tasks.what: Create src/lib/lab-engine/agents/verification/__regression__/ with one fixture+assertion per DISTINCT guard, each named for and citing its triggering run, asserting the guard FIRES on the original bad input AND PASSES the corrected/grounded variant. Harvest the trigger inputs that already exist inline in the unit tests (8081e646, c9bc2056, b0d12b45, d838ed4e, 314d5f02, 73dfbc0d, Anura, harness-ramp-2e3adf77) and promote them into standalone, provenance-stamped regression cases. Minimum one case per guard: stripExemplarEchoes (8081e646 Ramp-OCR-in-Airtable), downgradeUnpermalinkedVerbatimQuotes (8081e646 4/5 non-permalink), scrubQuoteEmails (8081e646 employee email), stripPlaceholderSourceUrls, enforceNumericCoherence (8081e646 5,900-vs-7,300), scrubInternalJargon (8081e646 CHANNEL POLICY / 314d5f02), dropConfessedExemplarQuotes, stripUngroundedNamedEntityMetrics, redactUnsupportedNumericClaims, stripMisattributedQuoteAttributions, quote chrome/truncation/dedup (c9bc2056), source-liveness containment (c9bc2056/d838ed4e), isValidGroundedBuyerUnit (b0d12b45 role-in-name-field), claim-extractor self-authored-label + display-URL exemptions (Anura / harness-ramp-2e3adf77), structuralVerifier fabrication detection. Each case header states: guard, run id, the production symptom, why a value-read scorer would not catch it. This is the load-bearing safety net Phase 2's strangler diffs against.

tasks.acceptance: `npm run verify:phase0` runs freeze-drift + __regression__ + eval gate and exits 0 on a clean tree in <10s; AGENTS.md names it as the Phase-0 acceptance bar; the command appears in package.json scripts.

risks.risk: A byte-for-byte 'fail on any change' freeze would fight the most active surface in the repo (37 commits/14d, evidence-support.ts edited 16x in 2 weeks) and get disabled within a day — defeating its purpose.
risks.mitigation: Design it as an ACKNOWLEDGEMENT gate, not an immutability lock: drift fails only until the editor regenerates+commits the manifest in the same commit. This converts every verifier edit into a reviewed, conscious act without blocking legitimate work. Captured as the P0 freeze task's CRITICAL DESIGN note.

risks.risk: The eval gate currently only exercises structuralVerifier; ~12 strip/gate functions that actually run in production are covered ONLY by hand-written unit tests. A Phase-0 freeze that hashes the files but leaves the eval gate blind to those functions gives false confidence — a refactor could keep hashes 'regenerated' yet silently weaken a strip.
risks.mitigation: P1 task extends the eval gate to run the production strip chain over the fabricated fixtures; the per-guard regression suite (P0) independently asserts each strip fires. The hash gate guards the bytes; the regression+eval suites guard the BEHAVIOR — both are required.

risks.risk: deck-ledger-gate.ts has zero production importers (scripts/tests only). Including it in the freeze without flagging this could make Phase 2 assume it runs on the live commit path and build the strangler around dead code.
risks.mitigation: The frozen-surface task explicitly records deck-ledger-gate's zero-prod-importer status in AGENTS.md so Phase 2 treats it as off-path; include it in the manifest (it is still verification logic) but with the caveat documented.

risks.risk: Regression fixtures that only assert numeric verdict counts (the current eval-gate shape: verifiedCount/unsupportedCount) can pass even when a strip mangles unrelated body content — the gate is blind to WHAT was stripped, only HOW MANY claims remain.
risks.mitigation: Per-guard regression cases must assert the actual post-strip body delta (quote dropped, URL relabeled, sentence removed), not just counts — and assert no-collateral (the grounded variant survives untouched). Specified in the regression-suite acceptance criterion.

openQuestions: Should src/lib/lab-engine/sections/required-evidence.ts be inside the frozen surface? It is the 2nd function the eval gate exercises (checkRequiredEvidenceClasses) but lives outside both verification dirs. Recommendation: include it, else the freeze is blind to half the existing eval gate.

Phase 0 acceptance per the brief is 'fabrication eval-fixtures + the thin floor'. The thin floor (quote-provenance/source-class + numeric-coherence recompute) is the Phase-1 deliverable from the verdict doc — does Phase 0 freeze the floor's two checks too, or only the legacy verification/ surface? The two source-floor checks live in the new core/source-floor.ts (Phase 2), not yet in verification/. Suggest Phase 0 freezes only what exists today and the floor gets its own snapshot suite when built.

Should the freeze gate run in a pre-commit hook (settings.json hooks exist per .claude/rules/hooks-and-automation.md) in addition to test:run, so drift is caught at commit time rather than test time? No CI exists, so the only enforcement points are local hooks + the test command.

Do we snapshot the FIXED gate-ordering chain in run-section.ts (~L5330-5510) as part of Phase 0? The order is load-bearing (a strip that runs before vs after dedup changes output), but run-section.ts is the file Phase 2 strangles — freezing it would fight Phase 2. Suggest capturing the order as an asserted sequence in a test rather than freezing the file.

risks.risk: The 8/8 value proof was scored on PROSE, but production must commit a TYPED body. The prose-to-typed bridge is unproven; GLM may fill enum-constrained typed rows worse than it writes free prose, silently regressing the proven value when forced into the schema (the exact DeepSeek-in-a-schema-fill-cage failure the re-platform set out to escape).
risks.mitigation: Make the bridge a structured-emit of GLM's OWN researched draft (not a fresh constrained generation), keep schemas loose where possible, and re-run zz-value-read on the RENDERED typed body (not the prose) for VoC + Demand before trusting the bridge. If typed-fill regresses value, consider a hybrid: typed rows for machine-rendered fields + a prose analysis field per section.

risks.risk: source-floor.ts is human-gated and the only liar-catcher. The proof validated the two checks on prose via an LLM repair pass; porting them to deterministic code over a typed body using the frozen verification/ primitives may not reproduce the same strip/hedge behavior (different representation, different claim-extraction surface).
risks.mitigation: Gate source-floor.ts against the typed synthetic-fabricated-* eval fixtures AND against typed bodies built from the proven repaired prose. Require 0 unsupported on clean fixtures and full strip on fabricated ones. Any verification/ file diff must be a byte-identical port (content-hash check from Phase 0).

risks.risk: Everything proven is Ramp-only (43+ runs, one company) on 2 of 7 sections. The agentic loop ability to fill the heavier typed bodies (competitorSet[], personas[], paid-media rows) and generalize across diverse subjects is unproven; a pilot ships the full 7-section deck.
risks.mitigation: Phase 3 cross-subject validation on 3 diverse subjects, all 7 sections, every section zz-value-read >=8, eval-fixtures green, before retiring the old path. Keep the strangler parallel-diff on until clean across >=3 subjects; any section the old path killed that the new commits gets manual review.

risks.risk: GLM rides Ollama Cloud which can 403 on the weekly cap for :cloud models. A pilot run mid-deck could hard-fail when auth lapses, and the spine has no provider fallback wired.
risks.mitigation: Provision the proper GLM API key (Zhipu/BigModel/z.ai) for production as the locked model decision states; createOpenAICompatible already supports a hosted baseURL + key, so this is a config swap. Re-check the endpoint returns 200 before each pilot run.

risks.risk: Strangling a 14,971-line monolith risks orphaning the section-specialized prepasses (VoC candidate prepass, competitor ad probe, buyer-persona mining) and the durable fact-ledger / evidence-pool writes that other parts of the system read. Cutting them could silently break downstream consumers.
risks.mitigation: Strangle-don't-swap: keep run-section.ts intact and authoritative until parallel diffs are clean on >=3 subjects; only then delete. Audit downstream readers of factStore/evidencePool before retiring those writes. Do not delete any monolith function until the spine demonstrably covers its behavior.

openQuestions: Should the typed body be produced by a second GLM structured-emit call (extra latency/cost) or a deterministic prose-to-typed parser? The former preserves model judgment but doubles model calls; the latter is brittle on free-form prose. The proof never decided this.

For the 5 unproven sections, can the agentic loop fetch competitor-ad and buyer-persona evidence itself via TOOL_CATALOG, or must the monolith specialized prepasses (runCompetitorAdProbeSteps, buyer-persona mining) be ported into the spine? Determines whether agentic-section.ts stays thin or absorbs prepass logic.

Does source-floor.ts run before or after the typed-body bridge — on prose (where the proof validated it) or on the typed body (what the eval fixtures expect)? Changes which frozen primitives it can reuse and what load-bearing-claim extraction operates over.

What is the agreed $/run budget and step cap per section for the live GLM loop, and is the existing SectionToolBudget cap (maxExternalLookups) the right bound, or does the agentic loop need a separate cost ceiling given it researches more freely than the boxed agent?

How do confidence and valueReadiness (artifact envelope fields renderers + rollup consume) get computed in the new single-pass finalize, now that the old multi-gate scoring is gone? Derive from floor strip-count, or a separate cheap judge?

audit.area: Strangle run-section.ts — parallel-run comparison harness + absent-reachability invariant (Phase 2 of the agentic re-platform)
audit.currentState:
run-section.ts is the single 14,971-line god-file (484KB) at src/lib/lab-engine/agents/run-section.ts. The public entry is runSection() (L14351). It dispatches to THREE resolution paths: runSectionViaStructuredBodyStream (L13495, the LIVE DEFAULT — isLabSectionStreamingEnabled L6052 returns true unless LAB_SECTION_STREAMING="false"), runSectionViaAnswerTool (L12696, used when LAB_SECTION_STREAMING=false), and a legacy structured path (L14351 body, for non-answerTool sections). answerToolSectionIds (L6027) selects which sections use the streaming/answer split.

The commit-vs-absent DECISION authority is evaluateCommittableAttempt() in src/lib/lab-engine/sections/committable-gate.ts (L83), returning a CommittableVerdict union (L49): minimumsFailed | requiredEvidenceMissing | hookReject | evidenceShortfall | committable. The two in-file call sites (L9119 streaming attempt-builder, L12097 structured attempt-builder) map this verdict to an AttemptResult (interface L4737: {output, artifact: ArtifactEnvelope|null, errors, plus six optional per-section *EvidenceGapArtifact fields, requiredEvidenceMissing, evidenceSupportShortfall}). artifact===null is the path toward ABSENT; a gap-artifact builder may substitute a degraded-but-real body before the final resolution.

The FINAL commit/absent resolution lives in the three path bodies (legacy L14550-14920, streaming L13360-13490, answer-tool L14240-14345). 'Commit' = saveCompletedArtifact (L4640) → store.saveArtifact (run-store.ts L498) → section status complete. 'Absent' = recordSectionFailure (L4595) → markSectionFailed (run-store.ts L619, status:'failed') + throw SectionRunnerError (class at L486). There are ~16 distinct throw/recordSectionFailure sites.

Two thin-floor verifiers already exist and are exactly the proven floor checks: numeric-coherence.ts (gateProseNumbers L596, enforceNumericCoherence L803, buildNumericTruthIndex L505) and quote-admission.ts (evaluateQuoteAdmission L220, isAdmissibleQuote L281). The acceptance gate exists: src/lib/lab-engine/agents/verification/__evals__/verifier.eval.test.ts runs structuralVerifier over 15 fixtures (incl. synthetic-fabricated-{price,quote}.json, synthetic-buyer-fabricated-persona.json) asserting exact verified/unsupported counts.

The PARALLEL-RUN SUBSTRATE already exists: scripts/zz-full-run-harness.ts runs all 7 sections in-process via runSection() against a filesystem RunStore (createRunStore), corpus-only by default (FREE, no paid tools — allowedTools:[]), budget-gated (--max-runs 3 / --max-spend-usd 6, tmp/zz-full-run/_ABORT kill-file), and captures commit-vs-absent precisely: result.artifact written to <sectionId>.json on commit, throw caught and written to <sectionId>.error.json on absent (L303-340). zz-value-read.mjs is the deterministic+LLM value scorer wired to read those decks. The agentic harness scripts/zz-agentic-section.ts proves GLM-5.2 via Ollama agentic loop but emits FREE-PROSE markdown (body.md), NOT a typed ArtifactEnvelope.

For each (subject, sectionId): run the OLD path (runSection) AND the NEW path (agentic-section→finalize-section) on the SAME researchInput; emit per-section {oldDecision: 'commit'|'absent', newDecision: 'commit'|'absent'|'gapped', divergence, oldErrors, newReadinessReason}. Flag every case where old=absent but new=commit/gapped for MANUAL review (where a leaked fabrication would hide) and every case where new=absent but old=commit (regression). Write tmp/zz-strangler-diff/<subject>/diff.json + report.md.
tasks.acceptance: Running zz-strangler-diff on Ramp (corpus-only, free) emits a diff.json with both decisions per section and a human-readable divergence list; the budget kill-file (_ABORT) and --max-spend-usd gate are honored. No Supabase/Clerk/browser.
dependsOn: Build core/finalize-section.ts — single-pass draft→CommitDecision spine (no post-gate mutation)

tasks.title: Strangle runSection() — route through the new spine behind a flag, old path intact in parallel
tasks.what: In run-section.ts runSection() (L14351), add a LAB_ENGINE_SPINE env switch: when set, dispatch to a thin core/finalize-section-backed path; when unset, the existing three paths run unchanged. Do NOT delete the old resolution blocks yet (strangle-don't-swap). Keep saveCompletedArtifact / store.saveArtifact / event emission identical so commit persistence and UI are unchanged. The new path must emit the same ArtifactEnvelope + section-completed/section-failed events.
tasks.acceptance: With LAB_ENGINE_SPINE set, zz-full-run-harness.ts runs all 7 sections through the new spine and writes the same per-section.json + deck.json shape; with it unset, behavior is byte-identical to today (existing run-section tests stay green: test:run -- src/lib/lab-engine).
dependsOn: Build core/finalize-section.ts — single-pass draft→CommitDecision spine (no post-gate mutation)

tasks.title: HUMAN-FIREWALL CHECKPOINT: byte-identical verification/ port (content-hash gate) — gate the strangle PR
tasks.what: Before the strangle PR merges, add a content-hash assertion (Phase 0 artifact, consumed here): a test that hashes every file under src/lib/lab-engine/agents/verification/*.ts against a committed manifest and FAILS on any drift. The strangler MUST import verification verifiers unchanged — agentic-section/finalize-section/source-floor reference them, never edit them. The PR reviewer manually confirms the verification/ diff is empty.
tasks.acceptance: `git diff --stat origin/main -- src/lib/lab-engine/agents/verification/*.ts` is EMPTY on the strangler PR (excluding any new __tests__ snapshot additions); the content-hash test is green and would go red if a verifier byte changed. Reviewer sign-off recorded.
dependsOn: Strangle runSection() — route through the new spine behind a flag, old path intact in parallel

tasks.title: HUMAN-FIREWALL CHECKPOINT: absent-reachability invariant audit on live diffs
tasks.what: Run zz-strangler-diff across ≥3 DIVERSE subjects (not just Ramp). A human reviews EVERY divergence: (1) every old=absent→new=commit case is manually inspected for a leaked fabrication past the thin floor (the floor must have hedged/gapped it, not let an invented number through); (2) ZERO new=absent cases may exist except schema/parse-death/abort — any other new=absent is an invariant violation that blocks cutover. Capture verdicts in a report.
tasks.acceptance: A diff report across 3 subjects shows zero new=absent from content quality (only schema/parse/abort), and every old=absent→new=commit transition has a human note confirming no fabrication leaked. Acceptance gate (verifier.eval.test.ts 15 fixtures + source-floor tests) green on every committed body — NOT the value-read score.
dependsOn: Build the strangler comparison harness — old-vs-new commit/absent decision diff
Strangle runSection() — route through the new spine behind a flag, old path intact in parallel

tasks.title: Retire the old resolution blocks + the content-route-to-absent throws after clean diffs
tasks.what: ONLY after ≥3 subjects show clean diffs and the invariant audit passes: delete the three duplicated resolution blocks and the content-quality absent throws — getEvidenceGateFailureReason throws (L13476/L14332/L14735), the paid-media verifier hardFail throws (L14830/L14863/L14904), and the requiredEvidenceMissing throws (L13437/L14293). Remove the now-dead getEvidenceGateFailureReason / shouldRepairAttempt-coupled-to-gate logic. Make LAB_ENGINE_SPINE the default (or remove the flag). Keep schema/parse/abort throws.
tasks.acceptance: grep for getEvidenceGateFailureReason throw sites returns 0 in run-section.ts; full test suite + verifier.eval.test.ts green; zz-full-run-harness.ts on 3 subjects produces decks with no content-quality absent; run-section.ts LOC materially reduced.
dependsOn: HUMAN-FIREWALL CHECKPOINT: absent-reachability invariant audit on live diffs

risks.risk: The CLAUDE.md note that LAB_VERIFIER_MAX_UNSUPPORTED defaults to Infinity is STALE — the code default (defaultMaxUnsupportedAllowed) is now 0 (evidence-support.ts L57). Any plan that assumes the gate is currently off will mis-estimate how often sections hard-fail to absent today, and the strangler's value depends on this being the live behavior.
risks.mitigation: Treat the live default as 0 (verified in code). The strangler removes this gate entirely from the commit path; confirm no other caller of getMaxUnsupportedAllowed relies on Infinity before deleting.

risks.risk: The prose→typed-ArtifactEnvelope bridge (agentic-section.ts) is the only fully-unproven seam. zz-agentic-section.ts proved GLM writes good PROSE; it never produced a schema-valid envelope. A final structured pass could re-introduce the exact 'DeepSeek in a schema cage' failure the re-platform exists to escape (the model flattens its rich prose into thin schema fields).
risks.mitigation: Prove the bridge on ONE section before the full build (the design's own 'confirm on Demand' assignment). Prefer keeping the prose as the rendered body + a structured mirror for the renderers/value-read, rather than forcing the model to re-emit everything as schema fields.

risks.risk: The three resolution paths (streaming/answer/legacy) are near-duplicates but NOT identical (streaming has a richer gap-artifact escape hatch and a VoC deterministic-synthesis fallback the legacy path lacks). A single finalize-section spine could silently drop a path-specific behavior, changing a commit to an absent for one section type.
risks.mitigation: The strangler-diff harness must exercise sections from ALL three path classes (answerTool streaming, answerTool non-streaming, legacy structured) and flag any new=absent. Do not retire a path until its specific sections show clean diffs.

risks.risk: Everything proven so far is Ramp-only (43+ runs, one company). The agentic loop's tool-calling discipline (does GLM actually FETCH SpyFu numbers for Demand on company #2, or invent them?) is unverified cross-subject. A floor that only catches Ramp-shaped fabrications could pass a different company's invented numbers.
risks.mitigation: Phase 3 / the invariant audit requires ≥3 diverse subjects with frozen corpora before cutover; the acceptance gate is the fabrication eval-fixtures + thin floor, not the value-read score, specifically so cross-subject fabrication is caught deterministically.

risks.risk: requiredEvidenceMissing only has a gap-artifact builder for demand-intent (committable-gate + run-section L9181); converting it to 'gapped' for the other 5 sections requires either a per-section gap builder or a generic one. A generic 'gapped' body that doesn't match the section schema would itself fail to render.
risks.mitigation: section-readiness 'gapped' must produce a schema-valid degraded body per section; reuse the existing build*EvidenceGapArtifact builders where they exist and add the missing ones, verified against each section's Zod schema + the UI renderer.

openQuestions: Does the agentic loop need a structured FINAL pass (generateText + Output.object on the section schema) to produce the typed ArtifactEnvelope, or should the prose be the rendered body with a lighter structured mirror? This decides whether the bridge re-introduces schema-cage flattening — it is the single highest-risk design call in the strangle.

Is GLM-5.2 the writer for the strangle build, or Opus? The design doc A/B used Opus-4.8 (3→9); the locked model decision and the proof harness both use GLM-5.2 via Ollama (8/8). The provider is LAB_ENGINE_PROVIDER-driven so the spine is provider-agnostic, but the cost/latency budget and the floor's tuning differ between them.

Should LAB_ENGINE_SPINE be a per-section flag (strangle one section at a time, e.g. VoC first) or all-or-nothing? Per-section lets the diff harness retire paths incrementally and limits blast radius, but complicates the Paid-Media-consumes-committed-cores dependency.

What is the agreed $/run ceiling for the live cross-subject diffs? The strangler-diff harness runs BOTH old and new paths live, roughly doubling paid-tool spend per comparison run; the budget gate defaults (--max-runs 3 / $6) may need raising for a 2-path × 3-subject × 7-section matrix.

Does 'gapped' need a distinct persisted status (vs the existing complete/failed in run-store), or does it persist as complete-with-blockGap? The UI already renders blockGap-substituted bodies, so 'gapped' may map to complete — but the value-read ceiling logic distinguishes honest-gap (neutral) from absent (ceiling 2), so the persisted shape must preserve that distinction.

verify.area: Strangle run-section.ts — parallel-run comparison harness + absent-reachability invariant (Phase 2 of the agentic re-platform)

corrections.taskTitle: Build core/section-readiness.ts — the SectionReadiness state machine + absent-reachability invariant as code
corrections.issue: The acceptance criterion ('fromCommittableVerdict never returns kind:absent for ANY verdict kind; map evidenceShortfall/minimumsFailed/requiredEvidenceMissing/hookReject to gapped') re-enables the EXACT behavior the codebase already tried, audited, and quarantined. verifierDowngradeMode (a.k.a. gateRefutedOnly in committable-gate.ts L143) is the 'downgrade-not-delete, count only refuted claims' mode — it is enabled for ONLY BuyerICP (section-registry.ts L235) because a prior verdict report concluded it 'launders fabrications' (load-bearing comment at run-section.ts L5599: 'Non-BuyerICP sections do NOT run verifierDowngradeMode (see verdict report — it launders fabrications)'). A prior Codex adversarial review filed this as P0 (memory obs 40896: 'gateRefutedOnly Bypasses Fabrication Protection on Live Path'). A blanket verdict->gapped mapper IS gateRefutedOnly applied to all 7 sections.
corrections.fix: Make the invariant SAFE-by-construction, not blanket. The mapper must depend on whether the un-traced claim was first STRIPPED/RELABELED by source-floor (gap #2's check (a)/(b)). Ordering is load-bearing: strip-or-relabel the offending claim FIRST (preserving the strike/downgrade marker so it stays VISIBLE per verification/AGENTS.md 'Unsupported load-bearing claims must remain visible'), THEN map the residual to committable/gapped. evidenceShortfall maps to gapped ONLY for claims the floor neutralized; a refuted (not merely unsupported) claim must still hold the §5 ceiling. Reorder the dependency: section-readiness depends on source-floor, not the reverse (current dependsOn is backwards — see next correction).

corrections.taskTitle: Build core/source-floor.ts — productionize the two proven thin-floor checks as strip-or-gap (never kill)
corrections.issue: Two factual errors. (1) The dependsOn arrow is BACKWARDS: source-floor is the safety mechanism that MAKES the readiness downgrade non-laundering, so section-readiness must depend on source-floor, not source-floor on section-readiness. (2) The task frames both checks as net-new productionization, but the strip-or-gap machinery for BOTH checks is ALREADY LIVE in run-section.ts and already returns {body, stripped} (downgrade, not reject): numeric-coherence — enforceNumericCoherence (L5456), redactUnsupportedNumericClaims (L5474), gateProseNumbers (L5505/L5514), stripUngroundedNamedEntityMetrics (L5462); quote-provenance — isAdmissibleQuote filters via `continue` (L10432) and downgradeUnpermalinkedVocQuotes relabels to 'directional'. Effort L overstates net-new work.
corrections.fix: Re-scope to 'extract + unify the EXISTING live strip-or-gap calls into one core/source-floor.ts pass' (effort M, not L). The genuinely-new work is narrow: (a) generalize the quote-provenance relabel beyond VoC (currently VoC-only) so a Perplexity-synthesis 'quote' in any section is relabeled, and (b) make numeric-coherence's named-entity strip (currently paid-media-only, L5462 gated on sectionId==='positioningPaidMediaPlan') apply to all sections per the proven floor-check (b). Flip dependsOn: source-floor has NO dep on section-readiness; section-readiness depends on source-floor.

corrections.taskTitle: Build core/agentic-section.ts — productionize the GLM agentic loop + prose→typed-ArtifactEnvelope bridge
corrections.issue: Minor: the 'Provider wiring via LAB_ENGINE_PROVIDER (models.ts createOpenAICompatible GLM path)' is slightly aspirational. models.ts (L19-26, L242) supports only anthropic | deepseek-direct | deepseek-ollama — there is NO GLM provider value. GLM currently rides the deepseek-ollama transport by overriding DEEPSEEK_OLLAMA_MODEL_ID=glm-5.2:cloud (as the harness does at scripts/zz-agentic-section.ts L48). So either reuse deepseek-ollama with the model-id override (cheap, but mislabeled) or add a proper 'glm-ollama'/'glm-api' provider entry — the latter is needed anyway for the locked production GLM API key (Zhipu/z.ai) which is NOT a localhost Ollama endpoint.
corrections.fix: Add a sub-task: introduce a first-class provider entry in models.ts for GLM (one for ollama-dev, one for the production GLM API base URL/key) rather than overloading deepseek-ollama. The bridge itself (prose→typed envelope filling a 636-line / 76-field VoC schema) is correctly the XL crux — keep that. Confirm the bridge on ONE section (Demand or VoC) before the full build, per risk #2.

corrections.taskTitle: Build core/finalize-section.ts — single-pass draft→CommitDecision spine (no post-gate mutation)
corrections.issue: The acceptance ('feed 3 unsupported load-bearing claims + 2 numeric-coherence strikes → NEVER kind:absent') is correct ONLY if source-floor neutralized them first; otherwise it is the laundering path. Also 'It MUST NOT contain ... the requiredEvidenceMissing throw — those content routes to absent are deleted' is too blunt: requiredEvidenceMissing means a whole evidence CLASS is missing (e.g. zero personas, zero quotes), which is exactly when a gapped-but-honest body is right — but the gapped body must be schema-valid per section, and gap builders currently exist for all 6 sections under minimumsFailed yet requiredEvidenceMissing only builds the demand-intent gap (run-section.ts L9181-9202). Converting requiredEvidenceMissing to gapped for the other 5 needs those 5 builders wired into the requiredEvidenceMissing branch.
corrections.fix: Acceptance must assert the claims are HEDGED/RELABELED-then-committed (with strikes preserved/visible), not silently kept. Add explicit sub-work: wire the existing build*EvidenceGapArtifact builders (BuyerICP/Competitor/Offer/Market/VoC already exist at L4228/L2657/L2747/L2911/L2400) into the requiredEvidenceMissing branch, not just minimumsFailed — risk #5 names this but no task owns it.

corrections.taskTitle: HUMAN-FIREWALL CHECKPOINT: byte-identical verification/ port (content-hash gate) — gate the strangle PR
corrections.issue: Priority/effort fine, but this is a Phase 0 artifact (per the roadmap's own Phase 0 = freeze verification/ byte-for-byte). The audit lists it as a gap (#7, REAL — no freeze/hash/manifest test exists, confirmed) AND as a task here, but sequences it AFTER 'Strangle runSection()'. That is too late: an agent editing a verifier DURING the strangle build (before the gate exists) can launder a fabrication, which is the one thing the pilot cannot tolerate. The freeze gate must land BEFORE any core/ module that imports the verifiers is written.
corrections.fix: Move the content-hash freeze gate to the FRONT of the work (it is Phase 0, a hard prerequisite of every core/ task), not a pre-merge checkpoint after the strangle. dependsOn should be [] and every core/* task should dependOn it.

falseGaps: Gap #2 framing ('No prose→typed bridge' is true, but the implied claim that the thin-floor checks need to be BUILT is false for the strip-or-gap behavior): both proven floor checks already run LIVE and already downgrade rather than reject — numeric-coherence via enforceNumericCoherence/redactUnsupportedNumericClaims/gateProseNumbers (run-section.ts L5456-5514) and quote-provenance via isAdmissibleQuote (L10432) + downgradeUnpermalinkedVocQuotes. The net-new work is unifying + generalizing them beyond their current section scoping, not productionizing from scratch.

Gap #3 partially overstated: it says 'gap builders exist for demand-intent but not all sections.' For the minimumsFailed verdict, gap builders fire for ALL SIX sections (run-section.ts L9126-9177), and a substituted gap artifact DOES become the committed body (L9402-9411: attempt.artifact reassigned to evidenceGapArtifact when non-null and not aborted). The gap is narrower and accurate ONLY for the requiredEvidenceMissing verdict (L9181 builds demandIntentEvidenceGapArtifact alone). The blanket 'absent loses partial analysis' is mitigated for minimumsFailed today.
missedGaps: MISSED — the blanket 'verdict→gapped, never absent' invariant IS the already-rejected gateRefutedOnly/verifierDowngradeMode behavior. The codebase quarantined it to BuyerICP only because a verdict report found it LAUNDERS FABRICATIONS (run-section.ts L5599 comment; Codex P0 obs 40896). The audit's task-1 acceptance ('never absent for any verdict kind') re-enables it for all 7 sections without flagging this. This is the single highest-stakes correctness risk in the whole strangle and the audit does not name it. Reconciliation: the downgrade is only safe AFTER source-floor strips/relabels the un-traced claim; downgrade-and-keep is the laundering path.

MISSED — verification/AGENTS.md is a binding DOX contract that says 'Deterministic checks ... must not be softened to mask bad artifacts' and 'Unsupported load-bearing claims must remain visible to callers and tests.' The strangler's goal (don't hard-fail on unsupported claims) is in DIRECT tension with this rail. The plan must explicitly reconcile: the floor STRIPS/RELABELS and preserves the strike marker (visibility), it does not commit the claim as-clean. No task captures this contract reconciliation; a careless implementation violates the DOX rail the closeout pass enforces.

MISSED — distinguishing 'unsupported' from 'refuted' is load-bearing and unhandled. value-read (zz-value-read.mjs L20/L187 countRefuted) caps a deck at ceiling 4 for a REFUTED claim or deck-gate violation, vs ceiling 2 for absent and neutral for honest-gap. The strangler's 'never absent' rule must NOT turn a refuted claim (proven-false, e.g. a contradicted number) into a clean commit — that would launder a §5 violation into a passing deck. section-readiness needs a 'refuted → hold the ceiling / gapped-with-refutation-marker' branch, not blanket committable.

MISSED — no task covers the 'gapped' persisted-status decision, which openQuestion #5 raises but leaves unowned. run-store has only complete/failed (run-store.ts saveArtifact vs markSectionFailed). value-read's ceiling logic depends on the persisted shape distinguishing honest-gap (neutral) from absent (ceiling 2) from refuted (ceiling 4). If 'gapped' silently persists as plain 'complete', the value-read ceiling distinction is lost and a gapped section scores as a clean deliverable. This is a concrete persistence-shape task, not just an open question.

MISSED — the strangler-diff harness as specced cannot detect the most dangerous regression: a section where old=commit and new=commit but new's BODY contains a leaked fabrication the floor missed. The diff only compares the commit/absent DECISION (old vs new), not the committed CONTENT. The verifier.eval.test.ts fixtures + source-floor must run on EVERY new committed body in the diff (the audit gestures at this in the invariant-audit acceptance but the diff harness task itself only emits the decision tuple). Add: run structuralVerifier + source-floor on every newDecision=commit/gapped body and flag any residual unsupported/refuted count > old.

audit.area: GLM provider productionization (Ollama to GLM API key)
audit.currentState:
Provider resolved once per process in src/lib/lab-engine/ai/models.ts from LAB_ENGINE_PROVIDER. Providers: anthropic, deepseek-direct, deepseek-ollama. The deepseek-ollama branch (createDeepSeekOllamaSelection L508-554) is a GENERIC OpenAI-compatible transport: createOpenAICompatible with apiKey OLLAMA_API_KEY or literal ollama, baseURL DEEPSEEK_OLLAMA_BASE_URL or localhost:11434/v1, model DEEPSEEK_OLLAMA_MODEL_ID or deepseek-v4-flash. One handle reused for runner/repair/writer/review/strategy. openai-compatible v2.0.48 installed. Selection module-cached (L600-617) via lazy Proxies, so an env change needs a process restart (Vercel redeploy), not a hot swap. Proven 8/8 harness scripts/zz-agentic-section.ts (L47-52) builds the same provider inline with glm-5.2:cloud. Dispatch gates on checkSectionModelDispatchPreflight via lab-section-preflight.ts (500 on misconfig). Live calls thread an AbortSignal but set no maxRetries and have no 403/429 backoff. zz-api-doctor.mjs probes DeepSeek/Perplexity keys with secret scrubbing but omits any GLM key. CONFIRMED via z.ai docs: prod GLM API is OpenAI-compatible at https://api.z.ai/api/paas/v4/ (Bearer, model glm-5.2), path must NOT carry /v1 (404 otherwise) - a base-URL + key + model-id swap into the existing builder.</parameter>

corrections.taskTitle: Add 403/429 retry and backoff for the section provider
corrections.issue: Overstates the 429 problem. The audit claims 'a single 429 fails the section with no retry,' but no maxRetries is set on the section calls (section-agent.ts generateText/streamText L1261, L1301 — neither passes maxRetries), so the AI SDK v6 DEFAULT of 2 retries with exponential backoff is active, and the SDK default retry policy already covers 429/408/409/5xx. The genuine, narrower gap is 403 (Ollama weekly cap): 403 is a non-retryable client error under the SDK default, so it fails immediately, AND there is no typed cap error (grep found zero APICallError/statusCode/isRetryable handling in run-section.ts). Also the proposed fix 'set explicit maxRetries on run-section.ts L12582/L13073' is mis-located — those call sites (streamStructured / runAnswerToolWithStallGuard) do NOT accept a maxRetries param; retries live in section-agent.ts where generateText/streamText are actually invoked, OR via retry middleware wrapping the model in models.ts.
corrections.fix: Scope the task to: (a) keep the SDK default for 429/5xx, (b) add explicit handling for 403 (Ollama-cap) and persistent 429 → surface a TYPED provider-cap error, never ABSENT the section. Wire maxRetries / retry-middleware in section-agent.ts (the generateText/streamText call sites) or as a wrapLanguageModel middleware in models.ts (alongside the existing withLocalDevTools wrapper), NOT at run-section.ts L12582/L13073. Keep P1.

corrections.taskTitle: Correct provider/model labeling so telemetry names the writer as GLM
corrections.issue: Largely a FALSE GAP as written. The stated acceptance — 'getSectionRunnerModelId returns glm-5.2' and 'modelId glm-5.2 in telemetry' — is ALREADY satisfied by current code. createDeepSeekOllamaSelection (models.ts L536-537) sets metadata.modelId = modelId = DEEPSEEK_OLLAMA_MODEL_ID, and getSectionRunnerModelId() (L653) returns metadata.modelId. With DEEPSEEK_OLLAMA_MODEL_ID=glm-5.2, the real id already surfaces. The ONLY mislabel is the hardcoded metadata.provider:'deepseek-ollama' string (L536, L518, L528, L542).
corrections.fix: Reduce to a tiny P2: optionally add a human-readable provider label that reflects the configured model (or rename the provider metadata string), but do NOT claim getSectionRunnerModelId needs a fix — verify it already returns glm-5.2 and close. Effort stays S; value is low.

corrections.taskTitle: Provision z.ai GLM production API key and capture prod env config
corrections.issue: The acceptance proves the raw key works but does NOT prove the proven 8/8 quality survives the endpoint swap. The 8/8 blind proof ran on Ollama localhost with model id 'glm-5.2:cloud' (zz-agentic-section.ts L48) — a DIFFERENT model-id string and transport than z.ai's 'glm-5.2' over https://api.z.ai/api/paas/v4. A 200 from /chat/completions does not validate tool-calling/structured-output parity.
corrections.fix: Add a real one-section live A/B through the z.ai endpoint as acceptance (or split it into the missed P0 task below). A bare curl-200 is insufficient to trust client output.

falseGaps: Telemetry modelId mislabeling (task #6): getSectionRunnerModelId() already returns metadata.modelId which already equals DEEPSEEK_OLLAMA_MODEL_ID (glm-5.2). models.ts L536-537 + L653. Only the hardcoded provider:'deepseek-ollama' STRING is cosmetic; the modelId is already honest.

Partial false on gap #3 for 429: 'a single 429 fails with no retry' is wrong — AI SDK v6 default maxRetries=2 with exp backoff is active (no override anywhere in section-agent.ts), and the SDK default retries 429/5xx. The genuine residual is 403 (non-retryable client error) + absence of a typed cap error.

openQuestion 'enum rename = test and 6-field-sync churn': overstated. grep shows deepseek-ollama appears in only 3 files (models.ts + ollama-corpus-live.test.ts + models.test.ts), NOT the 6-place onboarding field-sync chain. The defer recommendation is still correct, but the churn cost is small.
missedGaps: P0 — Proven 8/8 quality is UNVALIDATED on the production endpoint. The blind 8/8 proof ran on Ollama localhost with model 'glm-5.2:cloud' (zz-agentic-section.ts L47-52); production task #1 specifies z.ai 'glm-5.2' over api/paas/v4 — different model-id string, different transport, different tool-calling/structured-output stack. This is the single sharpest pilot blocker and the audit buried it as openQuestion #4. Must be a P0 task: run one full section (VoC or Demand) through the z.ai endpoint and confirm it still tool-fetches and scores >=8 before any client run. This compounds with the Ramp-only caveat — z.ai parity AND a non-Ramp subject are both unproven.

P1 — The GLM/ollama path receives providerOptions: undefined. section-agent.ts isDeepSeekModel() keys on model.provider.startsWith('deepseek.') (L513-514), but createDeepSeekOllamaSelection builds the handle with createOpenAICompatible({name:'ollama'}) so model.provider is 'ollama...'. Result: getStructuredProviderOptions (L591) and getToolLoopProviderOptions (L516) both return undefined for GLM — no thinking-disable, no anthropic structuredOutputMode. Consistent with the harness (which also passed none), so NOT a regression, but it means the production structured-output decode path for GLM is entirely on createOpenAICompatible defaults and has never been exercised against z.ai's JSON-schema handling. Verify during the z.ai live A/B; if z.ai needs thinking-disable or a specific structured mode, isDeepSeekModel-style branching must be extended to the ollama/GLM provider.

P1 — No section-outcome assertion that a provider hard-fail (401/403/429-exhausted) surfaces as a visible ERROR, not a silent 'absent' or empty-but-committed section. This is THE pilot-trust invariant (matches the Phase 2 invariant: absent reachable ONLY from schema/parse death). The audit's retry task gestures at it ('does not silently ABSENT the section') but no task pins it with a test. For a tool producing real ad-budget decisions, a section that silently empties on a z.ai cap is worse than a loud failure. Add a test: provider 401/403 → section status = error (operator sees it), never absent/empty-committed.

risks.risk: All evidence remains Ramp-only. A section that hits 8 on Ramp (a quote-rich, ad-heavy, high-search-volume subject) may collapse on a thin-data B2B subject — exactly where fabrication pressure is highest. This is the verdict's own sharpest caveat and applies per-section.
risks.mitigation: Do not declare any section 'proven' on Ramp alone; gate the moat-extension claim on Phase 3's 3-diverse-subject validation. Pick at least one deliberately data-thin subject so the floor's strip-to-honest-gap behavior is exercised, not just its keep-the-strong-evidence path.

risks.risk: Paid Media validation is ordering-coupled: it can only be scored after the 6 sections commit AND are floor-repaired, so a regression in any upstream section invalidates the PaidMedia run. The harness has no committed-artifact plumbing today.
risks.mitigation: Build the synthesis harness to take the 6 repaired bodies as explicit file inputs (decoupled from a live full-deck run) so PaidMedia can be iterated independently; only the final Phase 3 cross-subject run needs the live committed chain.

risks.risk: Paid budget exhaustion. The 5 unproven sections + PaidMedia each need live GLM runs (data tools cost the same regardless of model) plus multiple blind-scoring workflows; prior sessions hit the $6 paid budget ceiling mid-section.
risks.mitigation: Reuse the existing frozen Ramp corpus excerpts (already routed for all 7) so only the live tool fetches cost money; batch each section's run once, persist transcripts, and run the blind-scoring on saved bodies. Honor the budget kill-switch (scripts/zz-value-read.mjs / budget gate) before each live run.

risks.risk: Ollama weekly cap can 403 :cloud models mid-run, producing a partial/absent body that could be misread as a section failure rather than an infra failure.
risks.mitigation: Curl localhost:11434/v1/chat/completions for 200 before every live run (it is 200 now); on 403, treat as infra-blocked not section-failed, and provision the proper GLM API key (Zhipu/z.ai) per the locked model decision to remove the cap risk before Phase 3.

openQuestions: Does the thin floor need a THIRD deterministic check beyond quote-provenance + numeric-coherence to cover persona-laundering (BuyerICP), TAM/valuation-as-TAM (Market), and cross-section laundering (PaidMedia)? The floor-audit ledger task should answer this before Phase 2 commits to a two-check floor.

Recommended build/validation ORDER: (1) re-confirm VoC+Demand still 8 on the spine, then research sections by ascending risk/dependency — Competitor (tool-heavy but ad-evidence is well-defended), Offer (4 tools, lower numeric risk), Market (TAM-invention risk), BuyerICP (laundering + below-8 ceiling, feeds PaidMedia) — then (2) Paid Media LAST (depends on all 6 committed+repaired). Should BuyerICP be pulled earlier precisely because it is the riskiest and gates PaidMedia, even though it is harder?

For the numeric-recompute floor: is strip-or-hedge (never auto-correct) acceptable to operators, or does a stripped ratio degrade the value-read below 8? The repaired Demand body chose to recompute+show-derivation (15.9%) rather than strip — but that required an LLM. Can a deterministic floor achieve the same value without reintroducing fabrication risk?

Is the production GLM model decision (glm-5.2:cloud via Ollama for dev, proper GLM API key for prod) stable enough to run the 3-subject Phase 3 validation, or should the Sonnet comparison arm (verdict's open suggestion, ~$8/deck) be run on the arithmetic-heavy sections (Demand, Market, PaidMedia) before locking GLM as sole writer?

PaidMedia's keyword_ad_probe returns organic/ad counts and top_organic URLs but NO advertiser identity — does the section have ANY legitimate route to name a bidder, or must every named-bidder claim across the whole deck be hedged by construction? If so, the floor's bidder check is a blanket rule, not a per-claim trace.

verify.area: Extend the agentic 8/8 moat from VoC+Demand to all 7 deliverables under the agentic spine + thin floor

corrections.taskTitle: (currentState / all tasks) — output-format impedance mismatch is unaddressed
corrections.issue: The harness emits PROSE markdown (scripts/zz-agentic-section.ts L329 writes result.text to body.md; L78 system prompt explicitly orders 'No JSON, no schema fields'). But the entire existing verification stack and the Phase-0 oracle operate on STRUCTURED SectionOutput bodies: verifier.eval.test.ts (L40 asserts 15) runs against fixture.body which is a typed dict (painLanguage.quotes[].verbatimText), and structural-verifier.ts takes body:unknown as a structured object. The verdict's 'thin floor' was applied by an LLM workflow to markdown, NOT by code gates to a structure. Every SKILL.md is written to FILL a schema (body.tamGapPosture, body.personaReality.personas, body.marketSize.signals) — directly contradicting the harness's prose-only instruction.
corrections.fix: Make output-format the FIRST decision of this work-area, before extending the harness. Either (a) the agentic writer must also emit the structured SectionOutput (then existing gates + the 15-fixture oracle apply directly and task #7 is coherent), or (b) the deterministic floor must be (re)built to operate on prose (then numeric-coherence.ts/quote-admission.ts cannot be 'extended' — they are net-new prose parsers, and the eval-fixture oracle does NOT protect agentic output at all). Task #1 and #8 must resolve this; as written they assume gates extend cleanly, which the code shows they do not.

corrections.taskTitle: Adversarial floor audit per section / risk: bidder-attribution
corrections.issue: The audit treats named-entity/bidder attribution as a uniform fabrication risk 'across the whole deck' and an open-question blanket-hedge. That is wrong for Competitor. keyword-volume.ts and keyword-ad-probe.ts carry NO advertiser field (Demand + PaidMedia risk is real), but the Competitor ad tools (adlibrary.ts, google-ads.ts, meta-ads.ts, linkedin-ads.ts, plus advertiser-match.ts) DO carry advertiser/page_name fields — Competitor has a legitimate tool-traced route to name advertisers.
corrections.fix: Scope the bidder/advertiser-attribution floor check to the keyword-tool sections only (Demand, PaidMedia). For Competitor, the check is 'advertiser name must trace to an ad-tool field' (a trace, not a hedge) — which is closer to the EXISTING competitor ad-evidence-from-tools defense. Do not impose a blanket hedge that would wrongly strip Competitor's legitimate advertiser names.

corrections.taskTitle: Quote-provenance / source-class gate (gap #4)
corrections.issue: The audit understates the difficulty. perplexity-research.ts tags its OWN output source:'Perplexity sonar-pro' (L36/L171), BUT the tool description instructs the model to 'cite the underlying source, never Perplexity itself' — so a laundered quote in prose points to trustpilot.com, NOT perplexity. A deterministic gate cannot detect the laundering by sourceUrl alone; it must cross-reference whether the quote TEXT appeared in a successful page-fetch/reviews-tool body vs only in a perplexity answer. source-liveness.isEntityContainedInLiveText (verification/source-liveness.ts L540) is the right primitive but requires capturing fetched page bodies in the transcript — confirm the harness transcript persists tool OUTPUT bodies, not just call names.
corrections.fix: Spec the quote-provenance check as text-containment against fetched bodies (reuse isEntityContainedInLiveText), and add a harness acceptance criterion that transcript.json captures full tool-result bodies for review/firecrawl calls so the gate has substrate. This is more than 'add a perplexity branch to quote-admission'.

missedGaps: BIGGEST MISS: output-format impedance mismatch. Agentic harness emits prose (body.md); existing verifiers + the 15-fixture Phase-0 oracle operate on structured SectionOutput bodies. The 'deterministic thin floor' is therefore NOT a simple extension of numeric-coherence.ts/quote-admission.ts — those run on structure. Until the agentic writer either emits structured output OR the floor is rebuilt for prose, tasks #3/#4/#6/#7 rest on an unstated assumption. This must be the first decision, not a downstream detail.

DETERMINISTIC GROUNDING-PREPASS LOSS: the OLD path runs a deterministic persona/segment grounding prepass (run-section.ts L1269 buyer-persona-champions, L11556 mineSegmentEvidenceCandidates, plus buyer-icp-acquisition-ledger / case-study-mining / personaCompanyReconcilesWithSubject) that MINES grounded leads and FEEDS them into the prompt as 'SEGMENT-EVIDENCE leads' (BuyerICP SKILL.md L72 depends on this prepass existing). The agentic harness does none of this — it hands GLM raw tools+prompt. The audit mentions 'preserve equivalent grounding' in keyFiles but does NOT make it a P0 task. Without the prepass, BuyerICP's verbatim-segmentLabel grounding (its primary laundering defense) has no source spans to anchor to. This is a concrete reason BuyerICP may land below 8 agentically that the audit's risk note does not pin to the missing prepass.

SKILL.md ARE SCHEMA-AUTHORING INSTRUCTIONS, not prose rails. Task #1 says 'drive system prompt from SKILL.md role+iron-laws' but every SKILL.md is written to fill body.* schema fields (Schema anchors this skill must satisfy: body.categoryDefinition.prose, body.marketSize.signals, ...). Feeding them into a prose-only harness will produce friction (the model is told to author body.tamGapPosture but also told 'no schema fields'). The harness's hand-written prose goals for VoC/Demand exist precisely because the SKILLs don't translate cleanly to prose — so generalizing 'from SKILL.md' is harder than task #1 implies and needs a translation step per section.

NO BUDGET/COST GATE WIRED INTO THE HARNESS for the 5 new sections. The audit's budget risk is noted but the harness has no kill-switch; the project's budget gate (scripts/zz-value-read.mjs per memory) is separate. Competitor uniquely burns 6 + 24 ad-reserved lookups across 4 paid ad APIs (adlibrary/google_ads/meta_ads/linkedin_ads) — the single most expensive section to live-run. Sequencing Competitor 'first/early' (open-question recommendation) is the most budget-hostile order, contradicting the budget-conservation risk.

tasks.what: Write the cost model so the budget-sensitive owner can plan: data-tool costs are model-independent and itemized per section by allowedTools (Market: web_search+firecrawl+keyword_volume+perplexity; Competitor: web_search+firecrawl+reviews+ad/keyword probes; BuyerICP: web_search+firecrawl+perplexity; VoC: web_search+reviews+firecrawl+perplexity; Demand: keyword_discovery+keyword_volume[SpyFu]+web_search+perplexity; Offer: web_search+firecrawl+pagespeed; PaidMedia: keyword_ad_probe[SearchAPI]) PLUS the one-time corpus-gen per subject (Perplexity: 1 sonar-pro + 3 fan-out + 1 sonar-deep-research memo + optional repair). GLM writer marginal cost ~$0 (Ollama) today / GLM API later. Record measured $ from the budget ledger after the sweep. State the minimal-spend protocol: freeze corpus ONCE per subject, then re-run sections at $0 corpus-only for prompt/floor iteration, spend on --live only for the final scored deck.
tasks.acceptance: A short cost doc lists per-tool provider + per-section tool fan-in + measured corpus-gen and live-deck $ from _budget.json, and states the 'freeze once, iterate corpus-only, one paid scored deck' protocol.

tasks.title: Add a per-subject absolute calibration guard (don't gate non-Ramp against the Ramp anchor)
tasks.what: Confirm + lock the scorer behavior that non-Ramp runs use the ABSOLUTE bar (--min-score, default 8) and never the Ramp human-calibration anchor (CALIBRATION_RUN_BASENAME = harness-ramp-2e3adf77). Add a gate-test fixture for a non-Ramp run dir name to prove it routes to the absolute bar, so a genuinely-good different deck cannot fail as 'not calibrated'.
tasks.acceptance: zz-value-read.gate.test.mjs includes a case asserting basename !== anchor -> absolute-bar path (isCalib=false); a non-Ramp deck with all finalScores >=8 passes --gate.

risks.risk: GLM-agentic + thin floor is proven on Ramp ONLY (43+ runs, one company). Cross-subject behavior is genuinely unknown — a data-sparse subject (Fathom/dev-infra) may push GLM to fabricate where Ramp's data-richness masked it, or trigger many honest-gaps that depress value below 8.
risks.mitigation: This is exactly what Phase 3 exists to surface. Pick deliberately diverse subjects spanning data-rich/data-sparse and 3 buyer types; treat any sub-8 as a real signal about the writer/floor, not a calibration nuisance; the floor (liar-catcher) must hold, never be loosened to pass.

risks.risk: Output-shape gap (prose body.md, no verification object) silently caps every section at ceiling 7, making the >=8 gate unreachable and looking like a 'quality' failure when it is a plumbing failure.
risks.mitigation: Land 'Verify source-floor emits the verification verdict' BEFORE the sweep; add the harness warning for any committed section with verification===null so a missing verdict is loud, not silent.

risks.risk: Corpus-gen depends on the full live stack (dev+worker+Clerk+Supabase+Playwright) and Perplexity sonar-deep-research, which historically blew watchdogs and flaked; freezing 3 corpora could burn paid Perplexity budget on retries.
risks.mitigation: Build the browser-free freeze script (removes Playwright); freeze each corpus ONCE and reuse at $0; batch all 3 in one session; keep sonar-deep-research at reasoning_effort low per the learned-pattern; honor a one-call-no-loop budget guard.

risks.risk: Ollama weekly cap can 403 the glm-5.2:cloud model mid-sweep, aborting a paid live deck after the (model-independent) data-tool spend already happened.
risks.mitigation: Pre-flight curl localhost:11434/v1 before each live deck; provision the proper GLM API key (Zhipu/BigModel/z.ai) before the sweep so production reliability does not ride the Ollama cap; the data-tool spend is wasted only if the writer dies, so check writer health first.

risks.risk: The value-read LLM read is manual; a 3x7 sweep is ~21 reads and operator fatigue could make scores inconsistent.
risks.mitigation: Use the ceiling-only pre-gate to drop fabrication-capped decks for $0 before any human read; keep the verbatim rubric; consider 1 reader/section for the sweep (the 5-reader blind panel was for the one-time moat proof, not routine gating).

openQuestions: Should Phase 3's harness run GLM-agentic exclusively, or keep the strangler diff (new vs old runSection) per section so the retire-old decision has evidence on all 3 subjects? The RESUME handoff says retire old only after clean diffs on >=3 subjects — implying the harness must run BOTH paths per subject, doubling writer time (data-tool spend is shared since corpus is frozen).

What is the agreed $/deck and total Phase-3 budget ceiling? The harness default is $6/3-live-runs but a 3-subject GLM sweep + 3 corpus-gens will exceed that; the owner must set --max-spend-usd deliberately and reset _budget.json.

Are Fathom + Gusto + Resend/Plain the right 3, or does the owner have real prospective CLIENT subjects to use instead (the pilot goal is operators trusting output on REAL client subjects — validating on actual pilot prospects would be higher-signal than synthetic picks)?

Does corpus-gen require the production Vercel/worker, or can it run fully local (dev :3000 + local worker :3001 + keys)? The browser-free freeze script's feasibility depends on whether the dispatch corpus path can be invoked without Clerk/Supabase session — needs a 5-minute spike before committing the L estimate.

For Paid Media in the agentic path: it must consume committed upstream artifacts (the harness already injects committedPositioningArtifacts). Confirm the productionized agentic Paid Media reads them the same way, or it will fabricate a plan from nothing on a fresh subject.

corrections.taskTitle: Output-shape mismatch gap (gap #2)
corrections.issue: The gap's CONCLUSION is real (the agentic writer emits body.md/transcript.json/meta.json with NO verification object, so deterministicCeiling() caps it at 7 via the verification===null branch). But the EVIDENCE cited is factually false: it claims 'calibration anchor harness-ramp-2e3adf77 section JSONs have verification=false and would cap at 7.' They do not. 5 of the 7 sections carry full verification objects — positioningDemandIntent {verifiedCount:55,unsupportedCount:0}, positioningOfferDiagnostic {18,0}, positioningMarketCategory {24,9}, positioningBuyerICP {9,11}, positioningPaidMediaPlan {14,17}. Only the two ERRORED sections (Competitor, VoC, which are *.error.json) lack verification. The OLD runSection path DOES emit verification, the anchor IS correctly scored above 7, and the anchor is NOT capped at 7 wholesale as claimed.
corrections.fix: Re-anchor gap #2's evidence to the AGENTIC script's output shape only: scripts/zz-agentic-section.ts L329-331 writes body.md + transcript.json + meta.json and never a verification object; deterministicCeiling() (scripts/zz-value-read.mjs L262-274) returns ceiling 7 band 'unverified-directional' when artifact.verification===null. Drop the false claim about the anchor — it MISLEADS into thinking the OLD path also lacks verification.

corrections.taskTitle: Build a browser-free corpus-freeze script for an arbitrary URL
corrections.issue: OpenQuestion #4 treats 'can corpus-gen run without Clerk/Supabase session' as an unresolved 5-minute spike gating the L estimate. The code already answers most of it: research-worker/src/index.ts L151 exposes POST /run guarded by requireApiKey (validates process.env.RAILWAY_API_KEY at L44-52 — an x-api-key style check, NOT Clerk), and runDeepResearchProgram is a registered TOOL_RUNNER. A script can fetch the worker /run directly with the API key — no Playwright, no Clerk session. HOWEVER the audit overstates the win: /run is async (returns 202 accepted, L51) and the runner PERSISTS results to Supabase via getClient() (L503-541 region), so the freeze script still needs Supabase to retrieve the corpus. 'removes the Playwright wizard dependency so a corpus can be frozen with just dev+worker+keys' is correct only if 'keys' includes Supabase access for result readback.
corrections.fix: Resolve openQuestion #4 in the spec now (it is answered in code): corpus-gen needs dev-optional + worker:3001 + RAILWAY_API_KEY + Perplexity key + Supabase (for result readback) — NO Clerk, NO Playwright. Keep L estimate (the async-202 + Supabase-poll wiring is real work) but note the spike is already de-risked, so it will not slip to XL.

corrections.taskTitle: Make the harness drive the GLM-agentic writer (productionized) for all 7 sections
corrections.issue: The task (and Phase 2 generally) implies source-floor is net-new from scratch. Two of the floor's proven checks already have substantial implementations: src/lib/lab-engine/agents/verification/numeric-coherence.ts (27KB) and quote-admission.ts (14KB), each with a colocated __tests__ file. The audit never references these in any gap or task — it only names the to-be-created src/lib/lab-engine/core/source-floor.ts.
corrections.fix: Frame the source-floor work as a PORT/ADAPTER over existing numeric-coherence.ts + quote-admission.ts (+ provenance-gate.ts), not a from-scratch build. This is exactly the code Phase 0 freezes byte-for-byte, so source-floor should INVOKE the frozen modules, not reimplement them. Reduces the real net-new floor surface and tightens the Phase 0->Phase 2->Phase 3 contract.

corrections.taskTitle: Pick + freeze 3 diverse pilot subjects (corpora) in one batched paid session
corrections.issue: OpenQuestion #3 already flags it, but the task hard-codes Fathom/Gusto/Resend-or-Plain as if decided. The pilot's stated goal is 'operators trust output on REAL client subjects.' Synthetic picks validate the writer but do not validate the PILOT — a sub-8 on Gusto teaches less than a sub-8 on an actual prospect the operator will hand to a client.
corrections.fix: Make subject selection an explicit owner decision BEFORE this task runs, defaulting to real prospective pilot-client subjects where they exist, falling back to the diverse synthetic set only to fill the data-rich/data-sparse/buyer-type matrix. Keep the diversity constraint (none matching Ramp's finance+SpyFu-rich profile).

corrections.taskTitle: Generalize the full-run harness to load an arbitrary frozen subject
corrections.issue: Effort S and the loader logic are right, but the acceptance criterion 'fail loudly if briefInput missing' is the load-bearing detail and is under-emphasized. Verified: tmp/grill/airtable-fresh/_manifest.json has keys run_id,artifact_id,status,rollup,sections and NO briefInput; only ramp-fresh has it (51 fields, not the audit's stated 54). A naive loader would silently pass an empty {} brief to corpusToResearchInput and produce a garbage deck.
corrections.fix: Keep S. Elevate the briefInput-presence assertion to the PRIMARY acceptance gate (distinguishes a real frozen subject from a run-rollup manifest). Correct the field count from 54 to 51 (cosmetic, ramp-fresh/_manifest.json).

falseGaps: No gap is wholly false, but gap #2 contains a FALSE supporting claim: the calibration anchor's section JSONs do NOT have 'verification=false'. 5 of 7 (Demand, Offer, Market, BuyerICP, PaidMedia) carry real verification objects with numeric verifiedCount/unsupportedCount; only the 2 ERRORED sections (Competitor, VoC, stored as *.error.json) lack verification. The OLD runSection path emits verification and the anchor is scored above 7. The gap's real substance (the AGENTIC throwaway emits no verification object) stands — only the anchor evidence is wrong. (Cited: tmp/zz-full-run/harness-ramp-2e3adf77/positioning*.json + deterministicCeiling at scripts/zz-value-read.mjs L233-281.)
missedGaps: FLOOR IS A PORT, NOT NET-NEW (cost/effort blind spot): src/lib/lab-engine/agents/verification/numeric-coherence.ts (27KB) and quote-admission.ts (14KB) already implement the two proven floor checks (numeric-coherence + quote-provenance/admission), each with __tests__. The audit never references these — it frames source-floor as fully net-new. This understates how much is reusable and creates a Phase-0/Phase-2 contract risk: source-floor must CALL the frozen modules, not reimplement them, or the byte-for-byte freeze gate is meaningless.

CORPUS-FREEZE SUPABASE DEPENDENCY UNSTATED: the worker /run path (research-worker/src/index.ts L151, requireApiKey via RAILWAY_API_KEY, runner persists to Supabase at L503+) removes Playwright/Clerk but NOT Supabase — results come back through Supabase, not a sync HTTP body. The 'browser-free freeze' task says 'just dev+worker+keys' and never names the Supabase readback step, which is the actual integration work that justifies the L estimate. If the owner expects a fully local, Supabase-free freeze, the task as written cannot deliver it.

NO TASK VERIFIES corpusToResearchInput SURVIVES A NON-RAMP CORPUS SHAPE: every harness today runs Ramp's corpus, which is the only one corpusToResearchInput (src/lib/research-v2/corpus-to-research-input.ts) has ever parsed via researchInputSchema.parse(). A Fathom/dev-infra corpus with sparse reviews/sources could trip schema-parse or produce empty ResearchInput sub-blocks (the exact 'absent from schema/parse death' path the Phase-2 invariant cares about). The audit's tasks assume parse-clean (task #2 acceptance says 'corpusToResearchInput parses clean') but no task tests the SPARSE-corpus parse failure mode that data-sparse subjects are specifically chosen to stress — this is the most likely silent failure in the actual sweep.

NO BUDGET-LEDGER RESET/HYGIENE TASK: tasks #7 says 'raise --max-runs to >=3 deliberately for this sweep, then reset' and 'reset _budget.json' but no task OWNS the reset, and the harness appends a live run to the ledger at START (L121-125) so a crashed/aborted live deck still counts toward spend (fail-safe toward NOT spending). For a 3-subject sweep with possible Ollama-403 mid-run aborts, the ledger can lock out a legitimate retry. A small ledger-reset/forgiveness protocol task (or a --reset-budget flag) is missing and could block the sweep mid-session — directly pilot-blocking under the medium-severity Ollama-cap risk the audit itself names.

verified.label: Audit Reader UX — operator trust + finishability

audit.area: Audit Reader UX — operator trust + finishability for the supervised pilot
audit.currentState:
The deck renders through `src/app/research-v3/page.tsx` (state machine welcome→corpus→onboarding→sections→error) which mounts `src/components/research-v2/audit-reader-shell.tsx` (1488 lines) for the `sections` phase. The shell is poll-based (no token streaming): `src/lib/research-v2/use-audit-state.ts` polls `/api/research-v2/audit-state` every 2.5s and exposes per-section `workerStates` (status + phaseLabel) and committed `sectionsByZone` artifacts.

LAYOUT: a left reading column (one section at a time, max-w-860px) + a right `RunStatusCard` gutter showing `N/6` rollup, an elapsed clock, a shimmering active-phase line, and a clickable section navigator with `StatusIcon` per section. Mobile uses `MobileSectionSwitcher`. A bottom `AuditChatPanel` strategist composer spans the reader.

PER-SECTION RENDER (shell lines 1377-1447): four states — `complete` → `CompletedActivitySummary` (sources/tools/duration) + `TypedArtifactRenderer` body + `SourcesFooter`; `running` → `DraftingArtifactView` (skeleton + key-finding preview) or `RunningActivityView` (ActivityRail with the live phase feed); `error`/`aborted` → `ErrorStateBlock` with a Rerun button; `queued`/`locked` → placeholder. Paid-media gets a special `PaidMediaPlanTerminalPanel` with a Deck/Working toggle.

LIVE PROGRESS phase machine is real: `src/app/api/research-v2/audit-state/route.ts:40-43` defines `Compiling context | Reading sources | Drafting | Validating` (+ Committed), surfaced via `worker.phaseLabel` into the shimmer line and the running view.

PHASE-1 KEYSTONE (commit 4bea5e65): all 7 section renderers (`section-renderers/*.tsx`) call `deriveValueReadinessBadge(artifact.verifierSummary)` from `trust-tier.ts` and pass it to `VerdictHero` (`primitives/verdict-hero.tsx`), which renders ONE muted line: 'X of N blocks fully evidenced' — but only when `anyRich` is true (verdict-hero.tsx:22). This is the only trust signal that reaches the deck.

HONEST-DEGRADE rendering exists: `isDemandIntentHonestlyUnavailable` (demand-intent.tsx:99) collapses a fully-empty section to ONE `GapNote` ('Nothing here was fabricated — the section is reporting an honest gap') instead of five carpet-bombed gap panels; per-block empty states render `GapNote` (`primitives/gap-note.tsx`) with a 'How to close' sourcing hint. `GapNote` scrubs pipeline chrome via `reader-text.ts`.

FACADE STATUS (verified in code): (1) 'R1 floors → error' is FIXED — `run-section.ts:3491-3496` builds a schema-valid empty-but-honest body so deadline exhaustion degrades to an honest-gap COMMIT (needs_review/insufficient) instead of status=error. (2) 'Infinite auto-rescue' is FIXED — client cap `SECTION_ERROR_RETRY_CAP = 1` (use-audit-state.ts:25) + server per-zone row-count cap (run-lab-section/route.ts:242 'at most one auto-rescue per zone per run'). (3) 'Rerun destroys committed sections' is STILL TRUE.
