gaps.gap: The per-section honest trust tier (deriveTrustTier: Complete / Directional / Evidence-limited / Needs source check, with a one-line diagnostic like 'N load-bearing claims need an independent source') is fully implemented in trust-tier.ts but has ZERO render callers in the deck. Every finished section reads as uniformly 'Complete' regardless of how grounded it is.
gaps.evidence: grep for deriveTrustTier across src/components returns only trust-tier.ts itself (its own definition). audit-reader-shell.tsx lines 315-317 explicitly suppress verifier chrome. The only trust signal rendered is the muted 'X of N blocks fully evidenced' line in verdict-hero.tsx, gated on anyRich.
gaps.blocksPilotBecause: In a supervised pilot the operator's core job is to decide per-section whether to trust the output enough to put it in front of a client. With no visible per-section trust tier, a directional/evidence-limited section is visually indistinguishable from a fully-grounded one — the exact failure the GLM source-floor work is meant to make legible.

gaps.gap: Rerun wipes the committed section the instant it is requested, with no undo and no preview of the new result. reset_section_run_for_rerun nulls markdown/claims/sources and sets status=queued via on-conflict-do-update; the deck then shows the running skeleton. If the rerun commits an empty/worse/honest-gap result, the prior good deck section is gone.
gaps.evidence: supabase/migrations/20260601_claim_section_run.sql:209-218 (on conflict (artifact_id, zone) do update set status='queued', markdown=null, claims='[]', sources='[]'). rerun-section/route.ts:274 calls resetSectionRunForRerun before scheduling. audit-reader-shell.tsx:1225-1256 rerunSection just fires the POST; SectionActions Rerun button (states.tsx:87-95) has no confirm.
gaps.blocksPilotBecause: An operator who reruns a decent section hoping for better and instead gets a thinner result has irreversibly destroyed usable client deliverable content. This is the single biggest finishability/trust hazard and it is still live in code.

gaps.gap: The value-readiness line only appears when at least one block self-reports 'rich' (anyRich). A section that is entirely 'adequate'/'thin'/'gap' shows NO readiness line at all — so the weakest sections, where the operator most needs a caution, render with zero coverage signal.
gaps.evidence: verdict-hero.tsx:22 buildRichCountLine returns null when !valueReadiness.anyRich; the badge is otherwise dropped.
gaps.blocksPilotBecause: The signal is inverted relative to operator need: strong sections get a reassurance line, weak sections get silence. The operator cannot distinguish 'no readiness data' from 'low readiness'.

gaps.gap: There is no whole-deck completion / readiness summary an operator can read at a glance before exporting. The RunStatusCard shows N/6 + Done, but nothing aggregates per-section trust tiers ('4 sections complete, 2 directional, 1 needs source check') or flags which sections to sanity-check.
gaps.evidence: audit-reader-shell.tsx RunStatusCard (lines 715-811) renders only status icons + sublines (Complete/Running/Queued). ExecutiveBriefCard renders only when allSectionsTerminal && executiveBrief!==null (line 1113), and is a narrative brief, not a trust rollup.
gaps.blocksPilotBecause: An operator handing a deck to a client needs a single trust checklist of what is solid vs what to verify. Today they must open all 7 sections and infer trust from prose density.

gaps.gap: Copy-to-markdown (artifactToMarkdown) and Share strip ALL trust/provenance signaling. The exported markdown emits verdict + status + body + sources but no per-claim grounding, no trust tier, no honest-gap framing — so a copied section reads as more authoritative than the in-app deck.

The GLM source-floor work distinguishes quote-provenance and numeric-coherence, but the deck gives the operator no per-source-class affordance to sanity-check a verbatim quote or a named auction/bidder attribution against its origin.

risks.mitigation: Use the doc's non-alarming language and tone mapping (positive/neutral/caution, dot only when showDot), keep 'Complete' visually quiet, and validate the badge mix on the 3 Phase-3 cross-subject runs before locking copy. The fix is to be honest, not loud.

risks.risk: Making rerun non-destructive touches a security-definer Supabase RPC (reset_section_run_for_rerun) shared by the bounded auto-rescue path (run-lab-section/route.ts counts run rows to enforce the cap). Changing its insert/update semantics could break the rescue-cap row-count invariant and re-open the infinite-rescue facade.
risks.mitigation: Preserve the 'always mint a new research_section_runs row' behavior (the cap relies on it); only change the research_artifact_sections overwrite timing. Add a regression test that a rescued zone still has >=2 run rows and the cap still fires after the change.

risks.risk: The 'rerun destroys committed section' fix spans a DB migration + route + UI; a partial landing (UI confirm without the data-preserve) gives false safety — operator confirms, reruns, still loses the section on failure.
risks.mitigation: Land the data-preservation (migration + route) FIRST and prove it with the rerun route integration test; only then add the confirm dialog. Treat the confirm as the cherry, not the guard.

risks.risk: All UX work is being validated against Ramp only (43+ runs). Trust badges, the anyRich line, and source-class tags may render differently (or break) on subjects with sparser data, where most blocks are 'gap'.
risks.mitigation: Defer final copy/tone sign-off on these tasks to the Phase-3 cross-subject runs; build the renderers null-tolerant (legacy/absent metadata renders nothing) so a sparse subject degrades gracefully rather than throwing.

openQuestions: Does the new GLM source-floor write per-source-class and per-block value-readiness metadata into the SAME verifierSummary.computedTrust shape the deck already reads (deriveValueReadinessBadge / deriveTrustTier), or a new shape? The renderer wiring for tasks 1, 3, and 6 depends on this — confirm the artifact contract before building the badges.

For the non-destructive rerun: is the desired behavior 'keep old until new commits' (optimistic, simplest) or 'snapshot prior revision and offer restore' (full undo)? The migration changes differ materially; the pilot likely only needs the former.

Should the whole-deck trust rollup gate EXPORT (block/ warn on copy-all when sections are 'Needs source check'), or just inform? For a pilot that produces ad-budget decisions, a soft warning may be warranted but adds friction — needs an operator-workflow decision.

Confidence score is currently rendered only in strategy-brief-card.tsx, not per section (intentional per the 2026-06-11 decision). Confirm that decision still holds — i.e. the trust tier (not a raw 0–1 confidence) is the operator-facing signal — before adding any numeric confidence to the deck.

verify.area: Audit Reader UX — operator trust + finishability for the supervised pilot
missedGaps: AUTOMATED rerun can destroy a committed section with NO operator action — the audit frames rerun-destroys as an operator-initiated hazard only, but the destructive reset_section_run_for_rerun also fires from the client auto-retry path (use-audit-state.ts SECTION_ERROR_RETRY_CAP=1) and the server auto-rescue path (run-lab-section/route.ts:242, 'one auto-rescue per zone'). A section that committed an honest-gap body but is then auto-rescued goes through the SAME on-conflict-do-update wipe (migration 20260601:209-218). So task 2's data-preservation fix is MORE load-bearing than stated: it must protect against the auto paths, not just the UI Rerun button + confirm dialog. A confirm dialog does nothing for an automated rescue. This strengthens the case to land the migration/route data-preserve FIRST (which risk #3 already says) and means the acceptance test must cover an AUTO-rescue overwriting a committed body, not just a manual rerun.

The section NAVIGATOR (audit-reader-shell.tsx:765-789, the clickable section list + StatusIcon) is the operator's primary at-a-glance scan surface, and it carries ZERO trust dimension — StatusIcon (ui-kit/status.tsx STATUS_META) renders only run-status dots (complete/running/queued/error). Task 1 wires the trust tier into the per-section VerdictHero (in-body, requires opening each section) and task 4 puts a rollup in RunStatusCard, but NEITHER surfaces a per-section trust cue in the navigator the operator actually scans. For the supervised-pilot 'which sections do I sanity-check' job, the navigator dot is the cheapest high-value place to color by trust tone (caution amber when showDot). This is a small addition to task 1 or 4 but is genuinely missed.

Confidence value (0–1) is currently emitted into the COPIED markdown via the body-entry path UNLESS it is in COPY_META_KEYS — verify the export does not leak a raw numeric confidence that contradicts the trust-tier-only operator signal (audit open-question #4 raises this for the in-app deck but not for the copy path that task 5 edits). artifactToMarkdown filters COPY_META_KEYS (confidence/verification/review per shell:283-313 comment) so it is likely excluded, but task 5 EDITS this exact function and must assert confidence stays out while the trust tier goes in — otherwise the export gains a number the deck intentionally hides.

corrections.taskTitle: Make rerun non-destructive: confirm + preserve-on-fail (no silent loss of committed sections)
corrections.issue: The task scopes the destruction hazard to operator-initiated reruns (confirm dialog on SectionActions/ErrorStateBlock). But the same destructive RPC reset_section_run_for_rerun fires from TWO automated paths with no operator action: client auto-retry (use-audit-state.ts SECTION_ERROR_RETRY_CAP=1) and server auto-rescue (run-lab-section/route.ts:242). A confirm dialog cannot guard those. The acceptance test only covers a manual rerun.
corrections.fix: Make the DATA-preservation half (keep prior committed markdown/claims/sources until the replacement commits) the primary fix and explicitly cover the auto-rescue path: add a regression test that an auto-rescue of a committed honest-gap section does NOT blank it if the rescue fails. Keep the rescue-cap invariant intact (risk #2 is correct: still mint a new research_section_runs row so the row-count cap fires). Treat the confirm dialog as cosmetic for the manual path only. Re-rank effort closer to the high end of L given it must satisfy the rescue-cap row-count invariant.

verified.label: chat refinement + E2E operator run flow + client export

audit.area: Chat refinement + E2E operator run flow + client export
audit.currentState:
The operator run flow is fully wired and mostly solid. /research-v3 (src/app/research-v3/page.tsx) drives a reducer state machine: welcome → corpus → onboarding → sections → error. URL entry POSTs /api/research-v2/dispatch (sectionId: deepResearchProgram), then polls /api/journey/session every 2.5s to detect corpus completion, opens the OnboardingWizard, and on submit fires /api/research-v2/onboarding then fire-and-forgets /api/research-v2/orchestrate (run_id, executionMode: lab). Orchestrate (src/app/api/research-v2/orchestrate/route.ts, maxDuration 300, Clerk-auth) fans out all six positioning sections via Promise.allSettled and ACKs; the AuditReaderShell (src/components/research-v2/audit-reader-shell.tsx) renders typed cards per section from useAuditState polling, with a belt-and-suspenders auto-kickoff if it reloads mid-flow and finds no parent. Per-section status (queued/running/complete/error/aborted), live activity feed, elapsed clock, and a 7th locked Paid Media Plan (unlocks after 6/6) all render. Per-section rerun works from the shell (rerunSection → POST /api/research-v2/rerun-section) and from chat.

Chat refinement: AuditChatPanel (src/components/research-v2/chat/audit-chat-panel.tsx) is a useChat + DefaultChatTransport surface docked at the bottom of the reader, posting to /api/research-v2/chat (route.ts, 1297 lines). The two memory-flagged bugs are FIXED in code: multi-turn 400 is gone (passthrough part schema, lines 45-47) and chat-blind-to-sections is gone (loadCommittedSectionResults reads research_artifact_sections server-side, lines 276-315/1247-1256). The orchestrator (src/lib/research-v2/agents/positioning-orchestrator.ts) exposes 6 meta-tools: rerunSection (with refinement passthrough → researchInput.chatRefinement, rerun-section route lines 269-272), editSectionField (schema-validated surgical body edits via commitChatPatchAuto), draftStrategyBrief/reviseStrategyBrief, explainSource, summarizeArtifact. Side-effects apply in onFinish AFTER the stream closes; failures and change-reports are persisted as follow-up assistant messages.

Client export EXISTS and is more complete than memory suggested. Share button in the reader top bar (audit-reader-shell.tsx lines 1306-1323) → useSessionShare (src/hooks/use-session-share.ts) → POST /api/share → createV3SharedSession (src/lib/research-v2/share-snapshot.ts) snapshots all complete research_artifact_sections + executiveBrief into shared_sessions, returns a /shared/[token] URL and auto-copies it. /shared/[token] (src/app/shared/[token]/page.tsx) is public (middleware isPublicRoute) and renders V3SharedSessionView (src/components/shared/shared-session-view.tsx) which handles the research-v3 snapshot shape. Existing share links auto-refresh on every section commit via refreshV3SharedSessionSnapshotsBestEffort (src/lib/research-v2/supabase-run-store.ts line 1127). Per-section Copy emits markdown to clipboard (artifactToMarkdown). Print-to-PDF is supported via an @media print block (src/app/globals.css line 1500) plus no-print chrome classes and explicit audit-reader-shell + paid-media-deck print rules.

gaps.gap: Chat thread does not persist in the UI across reload or section navigation. AuditChatPanel's useChat starts with empty messages and there is no GET chat-history route; only the bottom panel exists. Server-side history IS written to audit_chat_messages and the route reads the last 6 for orchestrator context, but the operator sees a blank thread every time they reload or revisit a run.
gaps.evidence: src/components/research-v2/chat/audit-chat-panel.tsx lines 121-129 (useChat with no initialMessages); no GET handler in src/app/api/research-v2/chat/route.ts (only POST); history is written at route.ts lines 1043-1045 and read at 1262-1268
gaps.blocksPilotBecause: An operator refining a deck over a multi-message session loses the visible conversation on any navigation, making iterative refinement confusing and untrustworthy — they cannot see what they already asked or what the strategist already changed.

gaps.gap: Chat surgical edits (editSectionField, reviseStrategyBrief) do NOT refresh existing share links. Full section reruns refresh shares (via the commit path), but commitChatPatchAuto — the write path for chat edits — never calls refreshV3SharedSessionSnapshots, so a deck shared with a client before a chat edit shows stale content.
gaps.evidence: src/lib/research-v2/chat-write-through.ts has zero references to refreshV3SharedSessionSnapshots / share; contrast src/lib/research-v2/supabase-run-store.ts line 1127 which DOES refresh on section commit
gaps.blocksPilotBecause: Operator fixes a wrong TAM or competitor name via chat after sharing, client opens the link and still sees the error — silent divergence between the operator's reader and the client's shared deck erodes trust in client work.

gaps.gap: No whole-deck export. The reader has per-section Copy (markdown) and a Share link, but no 'copy entire audit', no 'download PDF/markdown', and no visible Print button. Print-to-PDF works only if the operator knows to use the browser's native Cmd+P, and the @media print rules are unverified against the live multi-section reader (only one section renders at a time in the DOM).
gaps.evidence: src/components/research-v2/audit-reader-shell.tsx — grep for copyAll/export/window.print/Print returns nothing; copyActive (line 1259) copies only activeTyped; print CSS exists at src/app/globals.css line 1500 but the reader shows one section at a time (active state), so Cmd+P likely prints a single section, not the deck
gaps.blocksPilotBecause: Delivering a finished deck to a client is the whole point of a pilot; the only multi-section deliverable today is a shared web link (good), but operators often need a self-contained PDF/doc to attach or present, and the current print path probably captures one section.

gaps.gap: Re-sharing creates duplicate share tokens instead of reusing/showing the existing one. useSessionShare.handleShare always POSTs /api/share which always INSERTs a new shared_sessions row; the hook never persists/reshows a prior shareUrl, and there is no 'manage shares' surface.
gaps.evidence: src/lib/research-v2/share-snapshot.ts createV3SharedSession lines 423-431 (unconditional insert, new token each call); src/hooks/use-session-share.ts only auto-copies on success, shareUrl state is component-local and lost on remount
gaps.blocksPilotBecause: Operator who clicks Share twice (e.g. after a reload) generates a second link; they may send a client the wrong/old token, and there is no way to see or revoke previously issued links — a real concern when the deck contains client-confidential positioning.

gaps.gap: Sections-phase failures have no top-level recovery. ErrorRecovery (research-v3 page handleRetry) only handles from:'corpus'; if the orchestrate fan-out itself fails to seed (e.g. corpus_not_ready 409, or the fire-and-forget POST is dropped) the operator is left on a sections view with queued-forever placeholders and only the auto-kickoff heuristic to save them.
gaps.evidence: src/app/research-v3/page.tsx handleRetry lines 749-786 only branches on state.from === 'corpus'; orchestrate kickoff is fire-and-forget (page.tsx lines 733-740, .catch only console.warns); auto-kickoff in audit-reader-shell lines 936-993 is the only fallback
gaps.blocksPilotBecause: For a supervised pilot on real client subjects, a fan-out that silently fails to start leaves the operator staring at perpetually-queued sections with no retry button — they cannot reliably get a run to complete without a page reload gamble.

gaps.gap: Chat shows tool success before the DB write actually lands ('false success', per memory 2026-06-15). The tool UI part flips to output-available (green Check) from the orchestrator tool result, but the real side-effect (rerun dispatch, editSectionField write) runs later in onFinish; a side-effect failure only appears as a separate follow-up assistant message on the next history refresh.
gaps.evidence: src/components/research-v2/chat/audit-chat-panel.tsx ToolStatusRow lines 108-109 renders 'done' on output-available; src/app/api/research-v2/chat/route.ts applies side-effects in toUIMessageStreamResponse onFinish (lines 1055-1084) AFTER the stream/tool part has already resolved; failures persisted as a separate ⚠️ message (lines 1090-1108)
gaps.blocksPilotBecause: Operator sees 'Editing section ✓' and trusts the edit landed; if the write failed (schema reject, CAS conflict) the correction silently didn't happen and the green check actively misleads — dangerous when the edited field feeds an ad-budget decision.

risks.mitigation: Run the full operator flow on the 3 diverse Phase-3 subjects before pilot, specifically watching the sections-phase failure/retry path and the share/export deliverable on each.

risks.risk: Share links are public and token-gated with no expiry/revocation; a client deck contains confidential positioning and ad-budget reasoning. Duplicate-token spawning (re-share) compounds this — old tokens stay live and unmanaged.

openQuestions: For the pilot, is the deliverable to the client a live /shared/[token] web link (already works, auto-refreshing) or a static file (PDF/doc)? This decides whether the whole-deck export task is P1 or can be deferred.

Does the Paid Media Plan (7th section) get included in the share snapshot? buildV3ShareSnapshot filters on isReaderSectionId — confirm positioningPaidMediaPlan is in READER_SECTION_IDS so the deck a client sees includes the media plan, not just the 6 positioning sections.
Should chat history be per-run only, or should the operator also see/resume threads across reruns of the same profile? Affects whether history hydration keys on runId alone.
Is there an expectation that an operator can edit/annotate the deck before sharing (operator notes, hide a weak section), or is the shared snapshot always a verbatim copy of all complete sections?

What is the acceptable behavior when an operator shares a run that is only partially complete (e.g. 4/6 sections)? createV3SharedSession throws v3_sections_not_found only at zero complete sections, so a partial deck is shareable today — is that desired for the pilot?

GLM forced into enum-constrained rows may regress the exact way DeepSeek-in-a-schema-cage failed.

This is a named P2 task, not an assumption.

topRisks.risk: The blanket 'never absent' invariant, if implemented as verdict→gapped for all verdict kinds, IS the already-quarantined gateRefutedOnly/verifierDowngradeMode behavior that a prior verdict report + Codex P0 found LAUNDERS FABRICATIONS (confirmed in code: it's enabled for BuyerICP only). Re-enabling it deck-wide would launder invented numbers into clean commits — catastrophic for an ad-budget tool.

blindSpots: VALUE-READ SCORER ITSELF IS UNFROZEN. The acceptance oracle for the whole re-platform is scripts/zz-value-read.mjs (deterministicCeiling bands: clean=10, unsupported=7, refuted/deck-gate=4, absent=2 — confirmed in the file header). It reads artifact.verification.{verifiedCount,unsupportedCount,claims}, the shape populated by structuralVerifier — which Phase 0 freezes — while the body-generation path is swapped underneath it. But zz-value-read.mjs's OWN band logic and countRefuted are NOT in the freeze surface and have no snapshot. A subtle change in how unsupportedCount is computed (or how the new path emits verification) silently re-bands every score, and the 8/8→pilot acceptance chain rests on an unpinned scorer. Add countRefuted/deterministicCeiling to the Phase-0 freeze + a fixture-pinned band test, or the oracle can drift without anyone noticing.

RAW LEAF NUMERICS ARE UNCHECKED — only ratios are. The single load-bearing empirical claim ('GLM fetched 39/39 SpyFu rows, does NOT invent numbers') was measured on ONE SpyFu-dense company. The floor's numeric-coherence recompute (verified to exist in numeric-coherence.ts) checks asserted ratios/percentages against cited operands, but a raw leaf number — a single CPC, a single monthly search volume — has no operands to recompute against. The undetectable failure: on a data-sparse subject GLM calls keyword_ad_probe, gets empty/thin results, and confabulates a plausible CPC that traces to a real (but empty-returning) tool call. No task asserts the floor cross-checks each load-bearing leaf numeric against the originating tool's RETURNED payload. This is the likeliest silent fabrication in the exact cross-subject case Phase 3 is designed to stress, and the floor as specced cannot catch it.

THE 14,971-LINE MONOLITH IS THE STRANGLE RISK, NOT core/. The plan treats run-section.ts as a clean strangle via the runSectionImpl seam (confirmed real at lab-section-job.ts:50) but the load-bearing pieces the new spine must REUSE are buried inside it: inferEvidencePoolEntryKind (L958), the FIXED gate-ordering chain (~L5283-5510), the BuyerICP persona/segment grounding prepass (~L1269/L11556), and 14+ SectionRunnerError throw sites. Each EXTRACTION (not rewrite) is a chance to subtly alter behavior the freeze gate cannot catch because these functions are NOT in verification/ and are NOT frozen. The plan freezes the 12 verifier files but leaves the entire extraction surface — the parts actually being moved — unprotected. A per-extraction characterization-test requirement (snapshot the extracted function's output on N inputs before and after the move) is missing.

NO HUMAN-CALIBRATION OF THE SCORER. The whole plan trusts 'blind value-read ≥8' as proof, but a single local-agent value-read on prose is itself a model judgment with no inter-rater check. The 8/8 was one scoring run. Before betting the pilot on ≥8 as the gate, the plan should pin at least one human-graded anchor per section type (operator reads 1 deck, scores it, compares to the agent's score) so the operators trust the SAME signal the gate uses. Otherwise the pilot's go/no-go bar is an unvalidated LLM-grades-LLM loop.

FROZEN VERIFIERS WILL FIGHT THE NEW TYPED SCHEMA. The freeze gate locks structuralVerifier/claim-extractor BYTE-FOR-BYTE, but P2.5 admits the typed schema may need a NEW sourceClass enum / directionalSignals block to carry hedged claims. The moment the body schema gains a field, the frozen claim-extractor (which walks the body to extract load-bearing claims) may not see the new field's claims — so a hedged claim in directionalSignals could escape extraction entirely and never reach the floor. The freeze-vs-schema-evolution tension is unaddressed: either the extractor must change (breaking the freeze) or new schema fields are invisible to the liar-catcher (laundering by schema). This is a direct collision between Phase 0 (freeze) and Phase 2.5 (schema work).
missingDependencies: P2.6 strangler-diff DEPENDS ON the typed-body bridge (P2.4) AND the value-read scorer emitting on the new path, but the plan runs zz-strangler-diff 'on Ramp (corpus-only, free)' — yet the diff must run structuralVerifier+source-floor on every NEW committed body, and source-floor consumes a transcript. The production run-section path captures step.toolResults but there is NO verified task that applySourceFloor(body, transcript, sectionId) can be FED a real production transcript at finalize time (the source-floor area finding flagged this as a P0 plumbing dependency the roadmap assumes away). Without transcript-at-finalize plumbing, the floor is proven only in the harness and the PILOT SHIPS UNFLOORED. This belongs as an explicit P2 task, not an assumption.

P0.3 (eval gate post-strip deltas) depends on the production strip CHAIN being importable in isolation, but those strips (enforceNumericCoherence, isAdmissibleQuote, downgradeUnpermalinkedVocQuotes, provenance-gate) currently run INSIDE run-section.ts's annotateEvidenceSupportReview (~L5283-5510), interleaved with state. Extracting them to run over a fixture is itself Phase-2 work (core/source-floor.ts), so P0.3 as specced cannot complete in Phase 0 without pulling the Phase-2 extraction forward. The dependency arrow points backward: the 'Phase 0 green' bar requires a Phase 2 extraction. Either P0.3 moves to Phase 2, or the source-floor extraction is pulled into Phase 0.

The factStore/evidencePool write-through audit (8 downstream readers incl strategy-brief/composer.ts, executive-brief.ts) is a P3 task gated before old-path deletion — but the NEW spine (P2.4) must START writing these from its first parallel run, or the strangler-diff comparison is apples-to-oranges (old path writes the pool, new path doesn't) and the diff cannot prove parity. The write-through preservation is a P2 build dependency of agentic-section.ts, not a P3 pre-deletion checklist item.

corpusToResearchInput sparse-corpus survival (P3 task) is a DEPENDENCY of the very first cross-subject run, but it is buried as a sub-bullet of the corpus-freeze task. If a sparse non-Ramp corpus trips researchInputSchema.parse, the entire deck dies at input-build before any section runs — this is a P0-for-Phase-3 gating test that should run BEFORE freezing 3 subjects, not after.
missingWorkAreas: NO ROLLBACK / DECK-INVALIDATION STORY FOR A POST-HOC FABRICATION DISCOVERY. The pilot produces ad-budget decisions a client acts on. If an operator (or a later run) discovers a leaked fabrication in an ALREADY-SHARED deck, there is no task for: revoking/expiring a share token (the chat-operator-flow finding confirms NO revoke/expire path exists anywhere), notifying that a number was wrong, or marking a section as retracted. For real client work this is a trust-and-liability gap, not a P2 polish item.

NO OPERATOR-FACING 'WHY WAS THIS STRIPPED/HEDGED' AFFORDANCE. The floor strips-or-gaps and 'preserves the strike marker (visible)', but no task makes the strip REASON legible to the operator at review time (e.g. 'this quote was relabeled to paraphrase because it traced only to a Perplexity synthesis answer'). Without it, an operator sees a hedged claim, can't tell if it's a real limitation or a floor false-positive, and either distrusts the whole deck or overrides the floor manually. The supervised-pilot's core job is the operator sanity-checking — and the plan gives them strip OUTCOMES but not strip RATIONALE.

NO DETERMINISTIC LEAF-NUMERIC PROVENANCE CHECK (the third floor check the plan explicitly declines to commit to). The 'is a third check needed?' question is deferred to a P3 ledger task, but BuyerICP persona-laundering, Market TAM/valuation-as-TAM, and raw-leaf-numeric confabulation are three DIFFERENT laundering classes that quote-provenance + ratio-recompute provably do not cover. Deferring the third-check decision to mid-Phase-3 means discovering a coverage hole AFTER the spine and floor are built around a two-check assumption — an expensive late pivot.

NO CONCURRENCY / SIMULTANEOUS-DECK behavior for 2 operators (the pilot is literally 2 operators). The module-cached provider selection, the fire-and-forget orchestrate kickoff, and the budget ledger (which appends a live run at START, per the full-deck-validation finding) are all untested under two decks running at once. Two operators kicking decks simultaneously could collide on the budget kill-switch or the auto-rescue cap. This is in-scope for the pilot definition but appears nowhere.
sequencingProblems: P0.3 (extended eval gate, PROMOTED to P0) is sequenced AFTER P0.2 (the freeze gate) but BEFORE the Phase-2 source-floor extraction — yet P0.3's acceptance ('run the production strip chain over fixtures, assert fabrication stripped') REQUIRES the strip chain to exist as importable functions, which is the Phase-2 deliverable. P0.3 cannot be green in Phase 0 without the Phase-2 extraction. This is a circular dependency between the Phase-0 acceptance bar and the Phase-2 build.

P1.1 (live-prove 8/8 survives the z.ai endpoint swap) is correctly P1, but it gates the ENTIRE re-platform's core premise (GLM is the writer) and sits behind owner provisioning a z.ai key (P1.0). If z.ai's glm-5.2 over api/paas/v4 does NOT reproduce the Ollama glm-5.2:cloud 8/8 (different transport, model-id, and the confirmed providerOptions:undefined path because provider string is 'deepseek-ollama' not 'deepseek.' — verified at section-agent.ts:514), the whole P2/P3 build is built on an unvalidated writer. P1.1 should be the VERY FIRST live action, before any P2 spine code, not parallel to it.

P4 (operator UX + E2E) 'can partly proceed in parallel after P2.5' per the open question, but P4's E2E proof (task: full browser flow on 3 subjects) DEPENDS ON P3's 3 frozen subjects existing AND the spine being default-on. The rerun-non-destructive and trust-tier-render tasks genuinely don't depend on the spine and should be pulled EARLIER (they de-risk the pilot independently and are confirmed-real bugs: deriveTrustTier has zero render callers, reset_section_run_for_rerun wipes markdown). But the E2E task is mis-sequenced as parallelizable when it is the true tail dependency.

The strangler 'retire old path only after ≥3 clean-diff subjects' (P3.4) is gated on Phase-3 live runs, but Phase-3 live runs are gated on the owner's budget ceiling (open question, unanswered). If the budget answer is 'one subject only', the ≥3-clean-diff invariant cannot be met and the old path can never be retired — leaving the 14,971-line monolith AND the new spine both live in the pilot, doubling the surface area an operator must trust. The budget answer is a hard precondition to the entire Phase-3 acceptance, not just a cost question.
underpricedRisks: COST/LATENCY PER FULL RUN IS UNQUANTIFIED AND THE STRANGLER DOUBLES THE WRITER COST. The agentic loop 'researches more freely than the boxed agent' (more tool calls, a second structured-emit GLM call for the typed bridge, plus the strangler runs BOTH old and new paths). For a 7-section deck × 2 paths × 3 subjects, with Competitor alone burning 6+24 ad-reserved lookups across 4 paid ad APIs, the realistic spend is multiples of the $6 harness default. No task produces a per-section $/latency budget BEFORE the live sweep. A pilot operator who waits 8+ minutes per section or hits a mid-deck budget kill will abandon the tool. This is named as an open question but should be a hard pre-Phase-3 measurement (run ONE full agentic deck, record $ and wall-clock per section).

GLM/OLLAMA→z.ai KEY DEPENDENCY IS A SINGLE POINT OF FAILURE WITH NO PROVEN FALLBACK QUALITY. The locked decision makes GLM the sole production writer. The rollback ('deepseek-direct, one env var') is a PROVIDER fallback, but DeepSeek-in-a-schema-cage is the exact path the re-platform exists to escape (it scored 1 and 3 on the same sections GLM scored 8). So the 'rollback' produces the broken output the pilot is trying to fix — it is an availability fallback, not a quality fallback. If z.ai is down/capped mid-pilot, the operator's only option is to ship the known-bad DeepSeek deck or nothing. The plan under-prices this: there is no second VALIDATED quality writer.

RAMP-ONLY OVERFIT IS UNDERPRICED EVEN AFTER PHASE 3. 43+ runs on one company on 2 of 7 sections. Phase 3 adds 3 subjects on all 7 sections — but 3 subjects is still a tiny sample for a tool making real ad-budget calls, and the acceptance ('per-section value-read ≥8') is the same LLM-grades-LLM loop that has only ever been calibrated on Ramp. The risk is not 'does it work on subject #2' but 'the floor's tuning (tolerances, source-class thresholds, hedge triggers) was set on Ramp's data shape and may false-negative on a finance/HR/dev-infra subject with different evidence density'. The mitigation (3 diverse subjects) tests the WRITER cross-subject but does not independently re-tune or re-validate the FLOOR's thresholds per subject.

THE MANUAL/DETERMINISTIC KEYSTONE-REPAIR GAP. The repaired Demand body that achieved 8 chose to RECOMPUTE+show-derivation (15.9%) rather than strip — but that required an LLM repair pass. The plan's floor is deterministic strip-or-hedge (never auto-correct), which the all-7-sections finding flags as possibly degrading value below 8 (a stripped ratio reads weaker than a corrected one). So the proven-8 path used a capability (LLM recompute-and-show) the production floor explicitly forbids. There is real risk the deterministic floor lands BELOW the 8 the LLM-repair achieved, and P2.5 ('re-prove on typed body') is the only checkpoint — if it fails, the entire floor design needs rework late in Phase 2.

FIVE SECTIONS PROVEN NOWHERE UNDER THE AGENTIC SPINE, AND THE TWO HARDEST (BuyerICP, PaidMedia) ARE STRUCTURALLY DIFFERENT. BuyerICP needs the deterministic persona-grounding prepass (confirmed buried in run-section.ts ~L1269/L11556) that the agentic harness does NOT run — without it BuyerICP's primary laundering defense has no anchor and may land <8. PaidMedia is a SYNTHESIS section consuming 6 committed artifacts with NO advertiser field — its quality depends entirely on upstream sufficiency and its bidder claims must be hedged by construction, an untested mode. The plan's single XL 'generalize to 5 sections' task collapses five distinct, unproven generation problems into one estimate. BuyerICP and PaidMedia each deserve their own validation gate, not a shared one.
critique.overallReadiness:
CONDITIONAL-GO as a build plan, but NOT yet pilot-ready, and the sequence has one fatal ordering bug. The plan is unusually well-grounded — I spot-checked ~15 load-bearing claims against the actual code and every one held: core/ does not exist; run-section.ts is 14,971 lines; committable-gate's evidenceShortfall→non-committable is real; verifierDowngradeMode is BuyerICP-only (section-registry L235); the eval gate runs ONLY structuralVerifier+checkRequiredEvidenceClasses and the synthetic-fabricated-quote fixture PASSES while the fabricated quote SURVIVES (verified the fixture asserts unsupportedCount:1 with the quote intact); deriveTrustTier has ZERO render callers; the GLM provider string mismatch (name:'ollama' → provider.startsWith('deepseek.')===false → providerOptions undefined) is real; the runSectionImpl strangler seam exists; no freeze/phase0 scripts exist. The plan correctly identifies its own sharpest caveats (Ramp-only, prose→typed bridge, the laundering trap of blanket verdict→gapped).

The SINGLE BIGGEST WEAKNESS is a circular sequencing dependency at the Phase-0/Phase-2 boundary that the plan does not see: the PROMOTED-to-P0 'extended eval gate asserts post-strip deltas' (P0.3) and the Phase-0 acceptance bar both REQUIRE the production strip chain to exist as isolated, importable functions — but that extraction IS the Phase-2 core/source-floor.ts deliverable. The strips currently live interleaved inside run-section.ts's annotateEvidenceSupportReview (~L5283-5510). So 'Phase 0 green' as specced cannot be reached without pulling Phase-2 extraction forward, AND the value-read SCORER that defines the entire acceptance ceiling (zz-value-read.mjs) is itself not frozen and can drift — meaning the whole 8/8→pilot proof rests on an unpinned oracle running over an un-extracted strip chain.

Second-order: the floor as designed checks ratios but NOT raw leaf numerics against tool payloads, which is precisely the fabrication a data-sparse non-Ramp subject will produce — and that subject is exactly what Phase 3 is meant to stress. Fix the P0/P2 circular dependency (move strip extraction into Phase 0 or move P0.3 into Phase 2), freeze the value-read scorer, add a leaf-numeric provenance check, run P1.1 (z.ai parity) as the literal first live action before any spine code, and split BuyerICP+PaidMedia into their own validation gates. With those, this is a strong supervised-pilot roadmap; without the P0/P2 fix, 'Phase 0 green' is a lie the plan would build three phases on top of.
